FILE: README.md
================================================
# Colibri Design System

A themeable Web Components library built with Lit, designed to provide a consistent and customizable design system for your web applications.

![Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@telesign/colibri)
![License](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/@telesign/colibri)
![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/@telesign/colibri)

## Features

- 🎨 **Themeable**: Light, dark, and custom themes supported
- 🔧 **Customizable**: Style tokens and CSS custom properties
- ♿ **Accessible**: Built with accessibility in mind
- 📱 **Responsive**: Mobile-first approach
- ⚡ **Performant**: Lightweight and fast
- 🛠️ **Web Components**: Framework agnostic

## Installation

```bash
npm install @telesign/colibri
```

## Quick Start

There are two ways to use this library, you can choose to load and register all of the components at once, or you can choose to load and register just component you need.

### Register all components

```javascript
import { registerAllComponents, registerColibriComponents } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { ColIcon } from '@telesign/colibri-icons';
import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';

registerAllComponents();
registerColibriComponents([ColIcon]);
```

### Register only needed components

```typescript
import { registerColibriComponents, ColButton } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { ColIcon } from '@telesign/colibri-icons';
import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';

registerColibriComponents([ColButton, ColIcon]);
```

Now you're ready to use the ColButton component:

```html
<div data-theme="telesign">
  <col-button color="primary">
    Send
    <col-icon name="send"></col-icon>
  </col-button>
</div>
```

> The `styles.css` file includes all necessary styling for the current themes. If you need to use a specific theme, you can import it directly:
>
> ```javascript
> // For default theme
> import '@telesign/colibri/styles/themes/theme-telesign.css';
> // For massive theme
> import '@telesign/colibri/styles/themes/theme-massive.css';
> // For bics theme
> import '@telesign/colibri/styles/themes/theme-bics.css';
> ```

## Browser Support

- Chrome/Edge ≥ 79
- Firefox ≥ 67
- Safari ≥ 14
- iOS Safari ≥ 14

## Documentation

For detailed documentation and examples, visit our [Storybook documentation](https://telesign.github.io/colibri-docs/).

### Technical Documentation

- [Component Guide](docs/components/develop-components.md)
- [Theming Guide](docs/theming/develop-theming.md)
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md)
- [Release Process](RELEASE_PROCESS.md)

## Contributing

We are not currently accepting external contributions to this repository. This is an internal project maintained by the Portals Team at Telesign.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## About Telesign

[Telesign](https://www.telesign.com) helps the world's largest brands connect, protect, and engage with their customers through powerful communication channels.

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start development server
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm test

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:watch

# Run tests with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run tests in headless mode (CI)
npm run test:headless

# Run tests with visual output
npm run test:visual

# Run tests in debug mode
npm run test:debug

# Run a specific test file
npm test -- --files="src/components/avatar/col-avatar.test.ts"

# Run tests matching a pattern
npm test -- --files="src/components/**/*.test.ts"

# Build for production
npm run build
```

---

Made with ❤️ by the Portals Team



================================================
FILE: cem.config.mjs
================================================
import { expandTypesPlugin } from 'cem-plugin-expanded-types';

export default {
  globs: ['src/components/**/*.ts'],
  exclude: ['src/components/**/*.styles.ts', 'src/components/**/*.test.ts'],
  litelement: true,
  outdir: './',
  packagejson: true,
  plugins: [expandTypesPlugin({ hideLogs: true })],
};

# Changelog for @telesign/colibri

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [0.13.0] - 2025-10-27

### Added

- New `col-step` component with full implementation, styles, and comprehensive testing (EOA-6505)
- New `col-stepper` component with horizontal and vertical orientation support and accessibility features (EOA-6505)
- New `col-collapse-header` component with expandable functionality, keyboard navigation, and comprehensive testing (EOA-6491)
- Enhanced `col-select` component with searchable/filterable functionality and autocomplete-like behavior (EOA-3905)
- New searchable mode for `col-select` with real-time filtering and keyboard navigation (EOA-3905)
- New empty state handling for `col-select` with customizable "No results found" message (EOA-3905)
- New support for user-disabled options in `col-select` component (EOA-3905)
- New clear functionality for `col-select` with improved state management (EOA-3905)

### Changed

- Enhanced `col-select` component with improved selection logic and state synchronization (EOA-3905)
- Enhanced `col-dropdown` component with improved JSDoc documentation and unit testing (EOA-3905)
- Improved form reset handler integration across selectable components (EOA-3905)
- Enhanced component testing coverage and reliability across new components
- Updated component architecture with better accessibility and keyboard navigation support

### Fixed

- Fixed component and testing definitions across multiple components
- Fixed indentation and code formatting issues
- Improved component integration and state management
- Enhanced accessibility attributes and ARIA integration

### Removed

- Removed `col-autocomplete` component - functionality integrated into enhanced `col-select` component (EOA-3905)

## [0.12.1] - 2025-10-10

### Fixed

- Fixed toast component and service errors during testing and build processes
- Fixed toast dismissal handling and animation cleanup for better user experience

- Fixed autocomplete component functionality and integration
- Fixed linter formatting issues across the codebase
- Improved toast component test coverage and reliability
- Enhanced toast service testing and error handling

- Migrated from Jest to Web Test Runner for better performance
- Upgraded testing libraries and infrastructure
- Improved test coverage and headless testing capabilities

### Fixed

- Fixed missing dev dependency issue
- Fixed import aliases mismatch across the project
- Fixed circular dependency issues
- Fixed module resolution problems
- Fixed build types configuration
- Fixed part name references in components
- Fixed path aliases configuration

### Removed

- Deleted component tokens (consolidated into other token files)
- Deleted duplicated colors function
- Removed release process documentation

### Added

- New `col-checkbox` component with full implementation, styles, and tests
- New `col-radio` component with full implementation, styles, and tests
- Added test examples and coverage for new components

### Changed

- Updated fonts management and documentation
- Improved theme-dependent global styles

### Fixed

- Fixed 'col-typography' host display issues
- Fixed 'col-button' host display issues
- Fixed styles for radio and checkbox components
- Removed missing font styles imports

  - Removed component list section

  - Removed support section

- New `col-avatar` component with full implementation, styles, and tests
- New `col-button` component with full implementation, styles, and tests

- New utility types, decorators, and configuration helpers
- New image asset: `colibri.png`
- Additional documentation and improved release process instructions

- Refactored and improved global styles, tokens, and configuration files
- Updated Badge component: improved styles, fixed bugs, and enhanced disabled state
- Improved and clarified README and documentation

- Various bug fixes in Badge and other components
- Fixed text alignment and semantic text variable issues in typography

FILE: index.html

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta
      name="viewport"
      content="width=device-width, user-scalable=no, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, minimum-scale=1.0"
    />
    <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="ie=edge" />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="/src/assets/images/colibri.png" />
    <title>Colibr&iacute;</title>
    <link
      href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:ital,opsz,wght@0,14..32,100..900;1,14..32,100..900&display=swap"
      rel="stylesheet"
    />
    <style>
      #body-app {
        margin: 40px;
      }
      #playground {
        margin: 20px 0px;
        padding: 40px;
        border: 1px solid #ccc;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body id="body-app" data-theme="telesign">
    <!-- DO NOT COMMIT OR PUSH ANY UPDATE ON THIS DOCUMENT -->

    <!-- You can remove this part while using the playground after making the needed updates -->
    <section>
      <col-typography variant="heading" element="h1">Welcome to Colibr&iacute;</col-typography>
      <p>
        If you'd like to use this space as a safe playground to test the component you're building,
        you'll need to make a few updates in the
        <col-typography variant="code" element="p">index.html</col-typography> file.
      </p>

      <col-typography variant="heading" element="h3">Register your component</col-typography>
      <p>
        The first thing you'll want to do is to register the component you're creating. For that
        please find the
        <col-typography variant="code" element="p">&lt;script&gt;</col-typography> tag below (in the
        <col-typography variant="code" element="p">index.html</col-typography> file) to import and
        register it.
      </p>
      <p>We've used ColButton as an example:</p>

      <col-typography variant="code" element="p">
        &lt;script type="module" &gt;<br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;// Replace ColButton with your component(s) <br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;import { registerColibriComponents, ColButton } from '@telesign/colibri';<br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;<br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;// and then in the register function: replace ColButton with your component(s)
        <br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;// make sure to keep ColIcon<br />
        &nbsp;&nbsp;registerColibriComponents([ColIcon, ColButton]);<br />
        &lt;/script &gt;
      </col-typography>
      <p>
        Then you can update the
        <col-typography variant="code" element="p">#playground</col-typography> section to see/use
        your component.
      </p>
    </section>
    <!-- ** You can remove this part while using the playground after making the needed updates -->

    <!-- UPDATE THIS SECTION TO INCLUDE YOUR COMPONENT -->
    <script type="module">
      // Replace ColButton with your component(s)
      import { registerColibriComponents, ColTypography, ColButton } from './src/index';
      import { ColIcon } from '@telesign/colibri-icons';
      import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';

      // Replace ColButton with your component(s)
      // Remember NOT to commit this file when pushing your changes!
      registerColibriComponents([ColIcon, ColTypography, ColButton]);
    </script>

    <col-typography variant="heading" element="h1"> Colibr&iacute; Playground </col-typography>

    <section id="playground">
      <col-button color="primary">
        Send
        <col-icon name="send"></col-icon>
      </col-button>
    </section>
  </body>
</html>

FILE: vite.config.ts
================================================
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import { resolve } from 'path';
import dts from 'vite-plugin-dts';
import { fontAssetsPlugin } from './tools/plugins';

export default defineConfig({
  define: {
    'process.env.NODE_ENV': '"production"',
  },
  build: {
    lib: {
      entry: resolve(__dirname, './src/index.ts'),
      formats: ['es'],
      fileName: () => 'index.js',
    },
    rollupOptions: {
      external: [/^lit/, /^@lit/, /^@telesign\/colibri-icons/],
      output: {
        globals: {
          lit: 'Lit',
          '@lit/context': 'LitContext',
          '@lit/reactive-element': 'LitReactiveElement',
          '@telesign/colibri-icons': 'ColibriIcons',
        },
        preserveModules: true,
        preserveModulesRoot: 'src',
        entryFileNames: chunkInfo => {
          return `${chunkInfo.name.replace('src/', '')}.js`;
        },
        assetFileNames: assetInfo => {
          if (!assetInfo.name) return 'assets/[name][extname]';

          if (/\.(woff|woff2|eot|ttf|otf)$/.test(assetInfo.name)) {
            return 'assets/fonts/[name][extname]';
          }
          if (/\.css$/.test(assetInfo.name)) {
            return 'styles/[name][extname]';
          }
          return 'assets/[name][extname]';
        },
      },
    },
    sourcemap: true,
    minify: 'terser',
    terserOptions: {
      ecma: 2020,
      module: true,
      compress: {
        passes: 2,
        dead_code: true,
        drop_console: true,
        drop_debugger: true,
      },
      mangle: {
        properties: false,
      },
    },
    assetsInlineLimit: 0,
    emptyOutDir: false,
  },
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      '@/components': resolve(__dirname, './src/components'),
      '@/styles': resolve(__dirname, './src/styles'),
      '@/config': resolve(__dirname, './src/config'),
      '@/utils': resolve(__dirname, './src/utils'),
    },
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    open: true,
    cors: true,
    hmr: {
      overlay: true,
    },
  },
  preview: {
    port: 4000,
    open: true,
  },
  plugins: [
    dts({
      insertTypesEntry: true,
      include: ['src/**/*.ts'],
      exclude: ['src/**/*.test.ts', 'src/**/*.spec.ts'],
    }),
    fontAssetsPlugin({
      sourceFontDir: 'src/assets/fonts',
      destFontDir: 'assets/fonts',
      fontExtensions: ['.woff', '.woff2', '.eot', '.ttf', '.otf'],
    }),
  ],
});

import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

        statements: 85,
        branches: 80,
        functions: 85,
        lines: 85,
      },
    },
  },
  resolve: {

      '@': resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      '@/styles': resolve(__dirname, './src/styles'),
      '@/config': resolve(__dirname, './src/config'),

FILE: web-test-runner.config.js
================================================
import { playwrightLauncher } from '@web/test-runner-playwright';
import { esbuildPlugin } from '@web/dev-server-esbuild';
import { fromRollup } from '@web/dev-server-rollup';
import alias from '@rollup/plugin-alias';
import { resolve } from 'path';

const isHeadless = process.env.TEST_HEADLESS === 'true' || process.env.CI === 'true';
const isDebug = process.env.TEST_DEBUG === 'true';
const browser = process.env.TEST_BROWSER || 'chrome';

const baseLaunchOptions = {
  headless: isHeadless,
  devtools: !isHeadless || isDebug,
  slowMo: isHeadless ? 0 : 100,
};

const browserConfigs = {
  chrome: {
    ...baseLaunchOptions,
    channel: 'chrome',
    args: isHeadless ? ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-dev-shm-usage'] : [],
  },
  chromium: {
    ...baseLaunchOptions,
    args: isHeadless ? ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-dev-shm-usage'] : [],
  },
  webkit: {
    ...baseLaunchOptions,
  },
};

export default {
  files: 'src/**/*.test.ts',
  nodeResolve: true,

  browsers: [
    playwrightLauncher({
      product: browser === 'chrome' ? 'chromium' : browser,
      launchOptions: browserConfigs[browser],
    }),
  ],

  plugins: [
    // Rollup alias plugin for module resolution
    fromRollup(alias)({
      entries: [
        { find: '@', replacement: resolve(process.cwd(), './src') },
        { find: '@/components', replacement: resolve(process.cwd(), './src/components') },
        { find: '@/styles', replacement: resolve(process.cwd(), './src/styles') },
        { find: '@/config', replacement: resolve(process.cwd(), './src/config') },
        { find: '@/utils', replacement: resolve(process.cwd(), './src/utils') },
      ],
    }),
    // ESBuild plugin for TypeScript compilation
    esbuildPlugin({
      ts: true,
      target: 'es2020',
      tsconfig: './tsconfig.json',
    }),
  ],

  coverage: !isDebug,
  coverageConfig: {
    include: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    exclude: ['src/**/*.test.ts', 'src/**/*.stories.ts'],
    threshold: {
      statements: 80,
      branches: 80,
      functions: 80,
      lines: 80,
    },
  },

  testFramework: {
    config: {
      timeout: isDebug ? 30000 : 10000,
    },
  },

  ...(isDebug && {
    open: true,
    manual: true,
    preserveSymlinks: true,
  }),

  logLevel: isDebug ? 'debug' : 'warn',
};

FILE: docs/components/develop-components.md
================================================
# Develop Components

## Component Usage

### Registration

Components need to be registered before they can be used. Colibri provides utility functions for this:

```typescript
import { registerColibriComponents, registerAllComponents } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { ColButton, ColTextField } from '@telesign/colibri';
import { ColIcon } from '@telesign/colibri-icons';
import '@telesign/colibri/styles/styles.css';

// Register specific components
registerColibriComponents([ColButton, ColTextField]);

// Register all components from @telesign/colibri package
registerAllComponents();

// Register external components like icons
registerColibriComponents([ColIcon]);
```

### Component Architecture

All Colibri components extend from either `ThemedComponent` or `FormComponent`:

```typescript
import { ThemedComponent } from '@telesign/colibri';

@Meta({ name: 'my-component', tokensName: 'myComponents' })
export class MyComponent extends ThemedComponent {
  static styles = [MyComponentStyles];

  // Component implementation
}
```

## Lit Integration

### Component Lifecycle

Colibri components extend Lit's lifecycle:

```typescript
class MyComponent extends ThemedComponent {
  protected updated(changedProperties: Map<string, unknown>) {
    super.updated(changedProperties);
    // Custom update logic
  }
}
```

### Property Declarations

Use Lit decorators for properties:

```typescript
class MyComponent extends ThemedComponent {
  @property({ type: String })
  label?: string;

  @state()
  private _value = '';
}
```

### Template Composition

Leverage Lit's template syntax:

```typescript
render() {
  return html`
    <div class=${classMap(this.classes)}>
      <slot name="prefix"></slot>
      ${this.renderContent()}
      <slot name="suffix"></slot>
    </div>
  `;
}
```

### Event Handling

```typescript
class MyComponent extends FormComponent {
  private _handleInput(evt: InputEvent) {
    this._dispatchInput(evt.target.value);
  }
}
```



================================================
FILE: docs/theming/develop-theming.md
================================================
# Theming System

## Introduction

Colibri provides an advanced theming system that allows for flexible and powerful customization of components.

## Component-Specific Theming

Components can receive theme overrides through the `tokens` property:

```typescript
const customTokens = {
  colors: {
    primary: '#FF0000',
    secondary: '#00FF00',
  },
};

html` <col-button .tokens=${customTokens}> Custom Themed Button </col-button> `;
```

## Theme Inheritance

Components automatically inherit theme context from their parents:

```typescript
html`
  <div data-theme="dark">
    <!-- Will use dark theme -->
    <col-button>Dark Button</col-button>

    <!-- Override to light theme -->
    <col-button theme="light">Light Button</col-button>
  </div>
`;
```

## Custom Token Generation

The token generation system supports:

1. Global tokens
2. Component-specific tokens
3. Theme variations
4. Dynamic token references

Example token structure:

```javascript
const tokens = {
  colors: {
    primary: '#007AFF',
    secondary: '#5856D6',
  },
  components: {
    buttons: {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--col-colors-primary)',
      textColor: '#FFFFFF',
    },
  },
};
```

## Local Themes

Local themes allow you to create component-specific theme variations without affecting the global theme. This is particularly useful when you need to:

1. Create isolated theme variations for specific components
2. Test different theme configurations
3. Implement component-specific theming without affecting other components

## Main Theme Development

When developing main themes, consider:

1. **Consistency**: Ensure theme tokens are consistent across components
2. **Accessibility**: Maintain sufficient contrast ratios and readability
3. **Flexibility**: Design themes to be easily extended and customized
4. **Performance**: Optimize theme token usage to minimize CSS complexity

## Font Management

### Font Standards

When working with fonts in Colibri, follow these standards:

1. **Format**: Use WOFF2 format for optimal performance and browser support
2. **Display**: Always use `font-display: swap` for better loading performance
3. **Fallbacks**: Include appropriate system font fallbacks for each font family
4. **Weights**: Define all necessary font weights and styles explicitly
5. **Naming**: Use consistent naming conventions for font files and families
   - Font CSS files should be named in kebab-case: `font-name.css`
   - Font family names in CSS should use Title Case with spaces: `'Font Name'`
   - Font file names should be in PascalCase: `FontName-Regular.woff2`
   - Font weight/style combinations should be appended with a hyphen: `FontName-Bold.woff2`, `FontName-Italic.woff2`

### Adding New Fonts

To add a new font to the system:

1. Place the font files in `src/assets/fonts/`
2. Create a new CSS file in `src/styles/fonts/` with the font-face declarations:

```css
@font-face {
  font-family: 'Your Font Name';
  src: url('../../assets/fonts/your-font.woff2') format('woff2');
  font-weight: normal;
  font-style: normal;
  font-display: swap;
}

/* Add additional weights/styles as needed */
@font-face {
  font-family: 'Your Font Name';
  src: url('../../assets/fonts/your-font-bold.woff2') format('woff2');
  font-weight: bold;
  font-style: normal;
  font-display: swap;
}
```

3. Update the theme configuration in `src/styles/themes/` to use the new font:

```javascript
export const yourTheme = {
  // ... other theme properties
  'primary-font': '"Your Font Name", system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif',
  'secondary-font': '"Your Font Name", monospace',
};
```

### Font Usage Guidelines

1. **Primary Font**: Use for main content, headings, and UI elements
2. **Secondary Font**: Use for code blocks, technical content, or as a complementary font
3. **System Fonts**: Include appropriate system font fallbacks for better performance
4. **Loading**: Fonts are loaded through the main `styles.css` file, no need for separate imports
5. **Performance**: Consider the impact of font file sizes on initial load time

### Best Practices

1. **File Size**: Optimize font files to reduce size while maintaining quality
2. **Character Sets**: Include all necessary character sets for your target languages
3. **Licensing**: Ensure proper licensing for all fonts used
4. **Testing**: Test fonts across different browsers and operating systems
5. **Accessibility**: Ensure fonts maintain readability at different sizes and weights

FILE: scripts/generate.test.ts
================================================
import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach, MockedFunction } from 'vitest';
import fs from 'fs/promises';
import { join } from 'path';

// Mock all external dependencies
vi.mock('fs/promises');
vi.mock('lightningcss', () => ({
  transform: vi.fn(() => ({
    code: Buffer.from('processed css'),
    map: undefined,
    exports: {},
    references: {},
    dependencies: [],
    warnings: [],
  })),
}));

// Mock internal modules
vi.mock('@/styles/themes', () => ({
  themes: {
    light: {
      primary: '#ffffff',
      secondary: '#000000',
      'primary-font': '"Inter", sans-serif',
      'secondary-font': '"Roboto Mono", monospace',
    },
    dark: {
      primary: '#000000',
      secondary: '#ffffff',
    },
  },
}));

vi.mock('@/styles/tokens', () => ({
  tokens: {
    spacing: {
      small: '4px',
      medium: '8px',
    },
    colors: {
      primary: 'primary-color',
      secondary: 'secondary-color',
      alpha: {
        'primary-10': 'value1',
        'primary-20': 'value2',
        'secondary-15': 'value3',
      },
      extended: {
        'custom-red': '#ff0000',
        'custom-blue': '#0000ff',
      },
    },
    typography: {
      'font-family': {
        primary: 'primary-font',
        secondary: 'secondary-font',
      },
      'font-size': {
        small: '12px',
        medium: '16px',
      },
      text: {
        heading: {
          h1: 'xl',
          h2: 'lg',
        },
        body: 'md',
      },
    },
  },
}));

vi.mock('@/styles/global', () => ({
  globalStyles: '/* global styles */',
  resetStyles: '/* reset styles */',
}));

vi.mock('@/styles/utils', () => ({
  alphaPercentToDecimal: vi.fn((val: string) => (parseFloat(val) / 100).toString()),
  hexToRgb: vi.fn((hex: string) => {
    if (hex === '#ffffff') return [255, 255, 255];
    if (hex === '#000000') return [0, 0, 0];
    if (hex === '#ff0000') return [255, 0, 0];
    if (hex === 'invalid-color') throw new Error('Invalid color');
    return [128, 128, 128];
  }),
  resolveTokenReferences: vi.fn((val: string) => val),
  generateAllGridUtilities: vi.fn((_tokens: any) => ''),
}));

vi.mock('@/config', () => ({
  TAG_PREFIX: 'col',
}));

// Import mocked modules for type checking
import { transform } from 'lightningcss';
import { alphaPercentToDecimal, hexToRgb, resolveTokenReferences } from '@/styles/utils';
import { themes } from '@/styles/themes';
import { tokens } from '@/styles/tokens';

describe('Generate Tokens Script', () => {
  let mockFs: {
    mkdir: MockedFunction<typeof fs.mkdir>;
    writeFile: MockedFunction<typeof fs.writeFile>;
    readFile: MockedFunction<typeof fs.readFile>;
  };
  let mockTransform: MockedFunction<typeof transform>;
  let mockAlphaPercentToDecimal: MockedFunction<typeof alphaPercentToDecimal>;
  let mockHexToRgb: MockedFunction<typeof hexToRgb>;
  let mockResolveTokenReferences: MockedFunction<typeof resolveTokenReferences>;

  // Console spies
  let consoleErrorSpy: ReturnType<typeof vi.spyOn>;
  let consoleLogSpy: ReturnType<typeof vi.spyOn>;
  let consoleWarnSpy: ReturnType<typeof vi.spyOn>;

  // Dynamic imports
  let generateModule: any;

  beforeEach(async () => {
    vi.clearAllMocks();

    // Dynamic import to avoid top-level await
    generateModule = await import('./generate.js');

    mockFs = {
      mkdir: vi.mocked(fs.mkdir),
      writeFile: vi.mocked(fs.writeFile),
      readFile: vi.mocked(fs.readFile),
    };

    mockTransform = vi.mocked(transform);
    mockAlphaPercentToDecimal = vi.mocked(alphaPercentToDecimal);
    mockHexToRgb = vi.mocked(hexToRgb);
    mockResolveTokenReferences = vi.mocked(resolveTokenReferences);

    // Setup console spies
    consoleErrorSpy = vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {});
    consoleLogSpy = vi.spyOn(console, 'log').mockImplementation(() => {});
    consoleWarnSpy = vi.spyOn(console, 'warn').mockImplementation(() => {});

    // Default mock implementations
    mockFs.mkdir.mockResolvedValue(undefined);
    mockFs.writeFile.mockResolvedValue();
    mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('mock font css content');
    mockTransform.mockReturnValue({
      code: Buffer.from('processed css'),
      map: undefined,
      exports: {},
      references: {},
      dependencies: [],
      warnings: [],
    });
    mockAlphaPercentToDecimal.mockImplementation((val: string) =>
      (parseFloat(val) / 100).toString()
    );
    mockHexToRgb.mockImplementation((hex: string) => {
      if (hex === '#ffffff') return [255, 255, 255];
      if (hex === '#000000') return [0, 0, 0];
      if (hex === '#ff0000') return [255, 0, 0];
      if (hex === 'invalid-color') throw new Error('Invalid color');
      return [128, 128, 128];
    });
    mockResolveTokenReferences.mockImplementation((val: string) => val);
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    vi.restoreAllMocks();
  });

  describe('Configuration', () => {
    it('should have correct CONFIG values', () => {
      const { CONFIG } = generateModule;
      expect(CONFIG.prefix).toBe('col');
      expect(CONFIG.fontAssetPaths.source).toBe('../../assets/fonts/');
      expect(CONFIG.fontAssetPaths.output).toBe('../assets/fonts/');
      expect(CONFIG.browserTargets.chrome).toBe(85 << 16);
    });
  });

  describe('File System Helpers', () => {
    describe('ensureDir', () => {
      it('should create directory successfully', async () => {
        const { ensureDir } = generateModule;
        mockFs.mkdir.mockResolvedValue(undefined);

        await ensureDir('/test/dir');

        expect(mockFs.mkdir).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/test/dir', { recursive: true });
        expect(consoleErrorSpy).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      });

      it('should handle directory creation errors', async () => {
        const { ensureDir } = generateModule;
        const error = new Error('Permission denied');
        mockFs.mkdir.mockRejectedValue(error);

        await ensureDir('/test/dir');

        expect(consoleErrorSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Error creating directory /test/dir:', error);
      });
    });

    describe('writeFile', () => {
      it('should write file successfully with string content', async () => {
        const { writeFile } = generateModule;
        mockFs.writeFile.mockResolvedValue();

        await writeFile('/test/file.css', 'css content');

        expect(mockFs.writeFile).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/test/file.css', 'css content');
        expect(consoleLogSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith('File written successfully: /test/file.css');
      });

      it('should write file successfully with Buffer content', async () => {
        const { writeFile } = generateModule;
        mockFs.writeFile.mockResolvedValue();
        const buffer = Buffer.from('css content');

        await writeFile('/test/file.css', buffer);

        expect(mockFs.writeFile).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/test/file.css', buffer);
        expect(consoleLogSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith('File written successfully: /test/file.css');
      });

      it('should handle file write errors', async () => {
        const { writeFile } = generateModule;
        const error = new Error('Write failed');
        mockFs.writeFile.mockRejectedValue(error);

        await writeFile('/test/file.css', 'content');

        expect(consoleErrorSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Error writing file /test/file.css:', error);
      });
    });

    describe('readFontCss', () => {
      it('should read font CSS without path adjustment', async () => {
        const { readFontCss, CONFIG } = generateModule;
        mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('original font css');

        const result = await readFontCss('inter', false);

        expect(result).toBe('original font css');
        expect(mockFs.readFile).toHaveBeenCalledWith(join(CONFIG.fontsDir, 'inter.css'), 'utf8');
      });

      it('should read font CSS with path adjustment', async () => {
        const { readFontCss } = generateModule;
        mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('url(../../assets/fonts/inter.woff2)');

        const result = await readFontCss('inter', true);

        expect(result).toBe('url(../assets/fonts/inter.woff2)');
      });

      it('should handle file read errors gracefully', async () => {
        const { readFontCss } = generateModule;
        const error = new Error('File not found');
        mockFs.readFile.mockRejectedValue(error);

        const result = await readFontCss('nonexistent');

        expect(result).toBe('');
        expect(consoleWarnSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          'Could not read font file for nonexistent:',
          error
        );
      });

      it('should use default adjustPaths parameter', async () => {
        const { readFontCss } = generateModule;
        mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('font css');

        const result = await readFontCss('inter');

        expect(result).toBe('font css');
      });
    });
  });

  describe('Value Conversion Utilities', () => {
    describe('escapeRegExp', () => {
      it('should escape all special regex characters', () => {
        const { escapeRegExp } = generateModule;
        expect(escapeRegExp('../../assets/fonts/')).toBe('\\.\\./\\.\\./assets/fonts/');
        expect(escapeRegExp('test[0-9]+')).toBe('test\\[0-9\\]\\+');
        expect(escapeRegExp('(hello|world)')).toBe('\\(hello\\|world\\)');
        expect(escapeRegExp('$^{}')).toBe('\\$\\^\\{\\}');
        expect(escapeRegExp('*?.\\')).toBe('\\*\\?\\.\\\\');
      });

      it('should handle empty string', () => {
        const { escapeRegExp } = generateModule;
        expect(escapeRegExp('')).toBe('');
      });

      it('should handle string without special characters', () => {
        const { escapeRegExp } = generateModule;
        expect(escapeRegExp('hello world')).toBe('hello world');
      });
    });

    describe('sanitizeFontName', () => {
      it('should sanitize font names with quotes', () => {
        const { sanitizeFontName } = generateModule;
        expect(sanitizeFontName('"Inter", sans-serif')).toBe('inter');
        expect(sanitizeFontName("'Roboto Mono', monospace")).toBe('roboto-mono');
      });

      it('should handle font names without quotes', () => {
        const { sanitizeFontName } = generateModule;
        expect(sanitizeFontName('Arial, sans-serif')).toBe('arial');
        expect(sanitizeFontName('Times New Roman, serif')).toBe('times-new-roman');
      });

      it('should handle single font name', () => {
        const { sanitizeFontName } = generateModule;
        expect(sanitizeFontName('Inter')).toBe('inter');
        expect(sanitizeFontName('"Inter"')).toBe('inter');
      });

      it('should handle font names with multiple spaces', () => {
        const { sanitizeFontName } = generateModule;
        expect(sanitizeFontName('Times   New   Roman')).toBe('times-new-roman');
      });
    });

    describe('getThemeFontNames', () => {
      it('should extract both primary and secondary font names', () => {
        const { getThemeFontNames } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          'primary-font': '"Inter", sans-serif',
          'secondary-font': '"Roboto Mono", monospace',
          'other-property': 'value',
        };

        const result = getThemeFontNames(theme);
        expect(result).toEqual(['inter', 'roboto-mono']);
      });

      it('should extract only primary font when secondary is missing', () => {
        const { getThemeFontNames } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          'primary-font': '"Inter", sans-serif',
          'other-property': 'value',
        };

        const result = getThemeFontNames(theme);
        expect(result).toEqual(['inter']);
      });

      it('should extract only secondary font when primary is missing', () => {
        const { getThemeFontNames } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          'secondary-font': '"Roboto Mono", monospace',
          'other-property': 'value',
        };

        const result = getThemeFontNames(theme);
        expect(result).toEqual(['roboto-mono']);
      });

      it('should return empty array when no font properties exist', () => {
        const { getThemeFontNames } = generateModule;
        const theme = { 'other-property': 'value' };
        const result = getThemeFontNames(theme);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should handle empty theme object', () => {
        const { getThemeFontNames } = generateModule;
        const result = getThemeFontNames({});
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });
    });
  });

  describe('CSS Processing', () => {
    describe('processWithLightningCss', () => {
      it('should process CSS successfully', () => {
        const { processWithLightningCss, CONFIG } = generateModule;
        mockTransform.mockReturnValue({
          code: Buffer.from('processed css'),
          map: undefined,
          exports: {},
          references: {},
          dependencies: [],
          warnings: [],
        });

        const result = processWithLightningCss('body { color: red; }', 'test.css');

        expect(mockTransform).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
          code: Buffer.from('body { color: red; }'),
          minify: false,
          sourceMap: true,
          filename: 'test.css',
          targets: CONFIG.browserTargets,
        });
        expect(result).toEqual(Buffer.from('processed css'));
      });

      it('should handle LightningCSS errors gracefully', () => {
        const { processWithLightningCss } = generateModule;
        const error = new Error('CSS processing error');
        mockTransform.mockImplementation(() => {
          throw error;
        });

        const inputCss = 'invalid css';
        const result = processWithLightningCss(inputCss, 'test.css');

        expect(consoleErrorSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          'Error processing test.css with LightningCSS:',
          error
        );
        expect(result).toEqual(Buffer.from(inputCss));
      });
    });
  });

  describe('Token Processing', () => {
    describe('getAlphaColorNames', () => {
      it('should extract alpha color names correctly', () => {
        const { getAlphaColorNames } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          colors: {
            alpha: {
              'primary-10': 'value',
              'primary-20': 'value',
              'secondary-15': 'value',
              'tertiary-25': 'value',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);
        expect(Array.from(result).sort()).toEqual(['primary', 'secondary', 'tertiary']);
      });

      it('should return empty set when colors.alpha is missing', () => {
        const { getAlphaColorNames } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: {} };
        const result = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);
        expect(result.size).toBe(0);
      });

      it('should return empty set when colors is missing', () => {
        const { getAlphaColorNames } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {};
        const result = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);
        expect(result.size).toBe(0);
      });

      it('should return empty set when colors.alpha is not an object', () => {
        const { getAlphaColorNames } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: { alpha: 'not-an-object' } };
        const result = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);
        expect(result.size).toBe(0);
      });

      it('should return empty set when colors.alpha is null', () => {
        const { getAlphaColorNames } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: { alpha: null } };
        const result = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);
        expect(result.size).toBe(0);
      });
    });

    describe('processTokens', () => {
      it('should skip alpha and extended colors', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          colors: {
            alpha: { 'primary-10': 'value' },
            extended: { custom: '#ff0000' },
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens, '--col', ['colors', 'alpha']);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);

        const result2 = processTokens(tokens, '--col', ['colors', 'extended']);
        expect(result2).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should process nested objects recursively', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          spacing: {
            small: '4px',
            nested: {
              tiny: '2px',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-spacing-small: 4px;');
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-spacing-nested-tiny: 2px;');
      });

      it('should handle typography text tokens specially', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          typography: {
            text: {
              heading: 'xl',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-text-heading: var(--col-typography-font-size-xl);'
        );
      });

      it('should handle color tokens with theme variables', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          colors: {
            primary: 'primary-color',
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-colors-primary: var(--col-theme-primary-color);');
      });

      it('should handle typography font-family tokens', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          typography: {
            'font-family': {
              primary: 'primary-font',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-font-family-primary: var(--col-theme-primary-font);'
        );
      });

      it('should resolve token references for other values', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        mockResolveTokenReferences.mockReturnValue('resolved-value');

        const tokens = {
          spacing: {
            medium: 'some-reference',
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(mockResolveTokenReferences).toHaveBeenCalledWith('some-reference');
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-spacing-medium: resolved-value;');
      });

      it('should handle null values', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          spacing: {
            nullValue: null,
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual(['  --col-spacing-nullValue: null;']);
      });

      it('should use custom prefix', () => {
        const { processTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          spacing: {
            small: '4px',
          },
        };

        const result = processTokens(tokens, '--custom');
        expect(result).toContain('  --custom-spacing-small: 4px;');
      });
    });

    describe('processSemanticTextTokens', () => {
      it('should process nested semantic text tokens', () => {
        const { processSemanticTextTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          heading: {
            h1: 'xl',
            h2: 'lg',
          },
          body: 'md',
        };

        const result = processSemanticTextTokens(tokens, '--col-typography-text', [
          'typography',
          'text',
        ]);
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-text-heading-h1: var(--col-typography-font-size-xl);'
        );
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-text-heading-h2: var(--col-typography-font-size-lg);'
        );
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-text-body: var(--col-typography-font-size-md);'
        );
      });

      it('should handle deeply nested objects', () => {
        const { processSemanticTextTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          section: {
            subsection: {
              item: 'sm',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = processSemanticTextTokens(tokens, '--col-typography-text', [
          'typography',
          'text',
        ]);
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-typography-text-section-subsection-item: var(--col-typography-font-size-sm);'
        );
      });
    });

    describe('processAlphaTokens', () => {
      it('should process alpha tokens correctly', () => {
        const { processAlphaTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          colors: {
            alpha: {
              'primary-10': 'value',
              'secondary-25': 'value',
            },
          },
        };

        mockAlphaPercentToDecimal.mockImplementation((val: string) =>
          (parseFloat(val) / 100).toString()
        );

        const result = processAlphaTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-colors-alpha-primary-10: rgba(var(--col-theme-primary-rgb), 0.1);'
        );
        expect(result).toContain(
          '  --col-colors-alpha-secondary-25: rgba(var(--col-theme-secondary-rgb), 0.25);'
        );
        expect(mockAlphaPercentToDecimal).toHaveBeenCalledWith('10');
        expect(mockAlphaPercentToDecimal).toHaveBeenCalledWith('25');
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors.alpha is missing', () => {
        const { processAlphaTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: {} };
        const result = processAlphaTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors is missing', () => {
        const { processAlphaTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {};
        const result = processAlphaTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors.alpha is not an object', () => {
        const { processAlphaTokens } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: { alpha: 'not-an-object' } };
        const result = processAlphaTokens(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });
    });

    describe('processExtendedColors', () => {
      it('should process extended colors correctly', () => {
        const { processExtendedColors } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {
          colors: {
            extended: {
              'custom-red': '#ff0000',
              'custom-blue': '#0000ff',
            },
          },
        };

        const result = processExtendedColors(tokens);
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-colors-extended-custom-red: #ff0000;');
        expect(result).toContain('  --col-colors-extended-custom-blue: #0000ff;');
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors.extended is missing', () => {
        const { processExtendedColors } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: {} };
        const result = processExtendedColors(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors is missing', () => {
        const { processExtendedColors } = generateModule;
        const tokens = {};
        const result = processExtendedColors(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });

      it('should return empty array when colors.extended is not an object', () => {
        const { processExtendedColors } = generateModule;
        const tokens = { colors: { extended: 'not-an-object' } };
        const result = processExtendedColors(tokens);
        expect(result).toEqual([]);
      });
    });
  });

  describe('CSS Generation', () => {
    describe('generateGlobalCss', () => {
      it('should generate complete global CSS', () => {
        const { generateGlobalCss } = generateModule;
        const result = generateGlobalCss(tokens);

        expect(result).toContain(':root [data-theme] {');
        expect(result).toContain('/* reset styles */');
        expect(result).toContain('/* global styles */');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-spacing-small: 4px;');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-colors-alpha-primary-10:');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-colors-extended-custom-red: #ff0000;');
      });
    });

    describe('generateThemeCss', () => {
      it('should generate theme CSS without fonts', async () => {
        const { generateThemeCss } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          primary: '#ffffff',
          secondary: '#000000',
        };
        const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>(['primary']);

        const result = await generateThemeCss(theme, 'light', alphaColorNames, false);

        expect(result).toContain(':root [data-theme="light"] {');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-theme-primary: #ffffff;');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-theme-primary-rgb: 255, 255, 255;');
        expect(result).toContain('--col-theme-secondary: #000000;');
        expect(result).not.toContain('--col-theme-secondary-rgb:');
      });

      it('should generate theme CSS with fonts', async () => {
        const { generateThemeCss } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          'primary-font': '"Inter", sans-serif',
          primary: '#ffffff',
        };
        const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>();

        mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('font css content');

        const result = await generateThemeCss(theme, 'light', alphaColorNames, true);

        expect(result).toContain('font css content');
        expect(result).toContain(':root [data-theme="light"] {');
      });

      it('should handle hexToRgb errors gracefully', async () => {
        const { generateThemeCss } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          primary: 'invalid-color',
        };
        const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>(['primary']);

        mockHexToRgb.mockImplementation(() => {
          throw new Error('Invalid color');
        });

        const result = await generateThemeCss(theme, 'test', alphaColorNames, false);

        expect(consoleWarnSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          "Could not convert color 'primary: invalid-color' to RGB components:",
          'Invalid color'
        );
        expect(result).toContain('--col-theme-primary: invalid-color;');
        expect(result).not.toContain('--col-theme-primary-rgb:');
      });

      it('should handle non-Error objects in hexToRgb catch block', async () => {
        const { generateThemeCss } = generateModule;
        const theme = {
          primary: 'invalid-color',
        };
        const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>(['primary']);

        mockHexToRgb.mockImplementation(() => {
          throw 'string error';
        });

        const result = await generateThemeCss(theme, 'test', alphaColorNames, false);

        expect(consoleWarnSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          "Could not convert color 'primary: invalid-color' to RGB components:",
          'string error'
        );
        expect(result).toContain('--col-theme-primary: invalid-color;');
        expect(result).not.toContain('--col-theme-primary-rgb:');
      });

      it('should use default includeFonts parameter', async () => {
        const { generateThemeCss } = generateModule;
        const theme = { primary: '#ffffff' };
        const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>();

        const result = await generateThemeCss(theme, 'light', alphaColorNames);

        expect(result).toContain(':root [data-theme="light"] {');
      });
    });

    describe('generateAllCss', () => {
      it('should generate complete CSS with all themes', async () => {
        const { generateAllCss } = generateModule;
        mockFs.readFile.mockResolvedValue('font css content');

        const result = await generateAllCss(tokens, themes);

        expect(result).toContain('font css content');
        expect(result).toContain(':root [data-theme] {');
        expect(result).toContain('data-theme="light"');
        expect(result).toContain('data-theme="dark"');
      });

      it('should handle themes with no fonts', async () => {
        const { generateAllCss } = generateModule;
        const themesWithoutFonts = {
          light: { primary: '#ffffff' },
          dark: { primary: '#000000' },
        };

        const result = await generateAllCss(tokens, themesWithoutFonts);

        expect(result).toContain('data-theme="light"');
        expect(result).toContain('data-theme="dark"');
      });
    });
  });

  describe('File Generation', () => {
    describe('generateCssFile', () => {
      it('should generate CSS file successfully', async () => {
        const { generateCssFile, CONFIG } = generateModule;
        const cssPromise = Promise.resolve('css content');
        mockTransform.mockReturnValue({
          code: Buffer.from('processed css'),
          map: undefined,
          exports: {},
          references: {},
          dependencies: [],
          warnings: [],
        });
        mockFs.writeFile.mockResolvedValue();

        const result = await generateCssFile(cssPromise, 'test.css');

        expect(result).toBe('test.css');
        expect(mockTransform).toHaveBeenCalled();
        expect(mockFs.writeFile).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          join(CONFIG.outputDir, 'test.css'),
          Buffer.from('processed css')
        );
      });
    });

    describe('generateCssFiles', () => {
      it('should generate all CSS files successfully', async () => {
        const { generateCssFiles, CONFIG } = generateModule;
        mockFs.mkdir.mockResolvedValue(undefined);
        mockFs.writeFile.mockResolvedValue();
        mockTransform.mockReturnValue({
          code: Buffer.from('processed css'),
          map: undefined,
          exports: {},
          references: {},
          dependencies: [],
          warnings: [],
        });

        const result = await generateCssFiles();

        expect(result).toHaveLength(4); // global.css + 2 theme files + styles.css
        expect(result).toContain('global.css');
        expect(result).toContain('theme-light.css');
        expect(result).toContain('theme-dark.css');
        expect(result).toContain('styles.css');

        expect(mockFs.mkdir).toHaveBeenCalledWith(CONFIG.outputDir, { recursive: true });
      });
    });
  });

  describe('Edge Cases and Error Handling', () => {
    it('should handle empty objects gracefully', () => {
      const { processTokens, processAlphaTokens, processExtendedColors, getAlphaColorNames } =
        generateModule;
      expect(processTokens({})).toEqual([]);
      expect(processAlphaTokens({})).toEqual([]);
      expect(processExtendedColors({})).toEqual([]);
      expect(getAlphaColorNames({})).toEqual(new Set());
    });

    it('should handle undefined and null values', () => {
      const { processTokens } = generateModule;
      expect(processTokens({ key: undefined })).toEqual(['  --col-key: undefined;']);
      expect(processTokens({ key: null })).toEqual(['  --col-key: null;']);
    });

    it('should handle complex nested structures', () => {
      const { processTokens } = generateModule;
      const complexTokens = {
        level1: {
          level2: {
            level3: {
              value: 'deep-value',
            },
          },
        },
      };

      const result = processTokens(complexTokens);
      expect(result).toContain('  --col-level1-level2-level3-value: deep-value;');
    });
  });
});



================================================
FILE: scripts/generate.ts
================================================
import fs from 'fs/promises';
import { join, dirname } from 'path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
import { transform } from 'lightningcss';
import { themes } from '@/styles/themes';
import { tokens } from '@/styles/tokens';
import { globalStyles, resetStyles } from '@/styles/global';
import {
  alphaPercentToDecimal,
  hexToRgb,
  resolveTokenReferences,
  generateAllGridUtilities,
} from '@/styles/utils';
import { TAG_PREFIX } from '@/config';

// =============================================
// Configuration
// =============================================
const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
const __dirname = dirname(__filename);

const CONFIG = {
  prefix: TAG_PREFIX,
  outputDir: join(__dirname, '../dist/styles'),
  fontsDir: join(__dirname, '../src/styles/fonts'),
  fontAssetPaths: {
    source: '../../assets/fonts/',
    output: '../assets/fonts/',
  },
  browserTargets: {
    safari: 13 << 16,
    ios_saf: 13 << 16,
    chrome: 85 << 16,
    firefox: 85 << 16,
    edge: 85 << 16,
  },
};

// =============================================
// File System Helpers
// =============================================
const ensureDir = (dir: string) =>
  fs
    .mkdir(dir, { recursive: true })
    .catch(error => console.error(`Error creating directory ${dir}:`, error));

const writeFile = (path: string, content: string | Buffer) =>
  fs
    .writeFile(path, content)
    .then(() => console.log(`File written successfully: ${path}`))
    .catch(error => console.error(`Error writing file ${path}:`, error));

const readFontCss = async (fontName: string, adjustPaths: boolean = false): Promise<string> => {
  try {
    const filePath = join(CONFIG.fontsDir, `${fontName}.css`);
    let content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf8');

    if (adjustPaths) {
      content = content.replace(
        new RegExp(escapeRegExp(CONFIG.fontAssetPaths.source), 'g'),
        CONFIG.fontAssetPaths.output
      );
    }

    return content;
  } catch (error) {
    console.warn(`Could not read font file for ${fontName}:`, error);
    return '';
  }
};

const escapeRegExp = (string: string): string => {
  return string.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
};

// =============================================
// Value Conversion Utilities
// =============================================
const sanitizeFontName = (fontDeclaration: string): string => {
  const primaryFont = fontDeclaration.split(',')[0].trim();
  return primaryFont.replace(/["']/g, '').toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-');
};

const getThemeFontNames = (theme: any): string[] => {
  const fontNames: string[] = [];

  if (theme['primary-font']) {
    fontNames.push(sanitizeFontName(theme['primary-font']));
  }

  if (theme['secondary-font']) {
    fontNames.push(sanitizeFontName(theme['secondary-font']));
  }

  return fontNames;
};

// =============================================
// CSS Processing
// =============================================
const processWithLightningCss = (css: string, filename: string): Buffer => {
  try {
    const { code } = transform({
      code: Buffer.from(css),
      minify: false,
      sourceMap: true,
      filename: filename,
      targets: CONFIG.browserTargets,
    });

    return Buffer.from(code);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Error processing ${filename} with LightningCSS:`, error);
    return Buffer.from(css);
  }
};

// =============================================
// Token Processing
// =============================================
const getAlphaColorNames = (tokens: any): Set<string> => {
  const alphaColorNames = new Set<string>();

  if (!tokens.colors?.alpha || typeof tokens.colors.alpha !== 'object') {
    return alphaColorNames;
  }

  Object.keys(tokens.colors.alpha).forEach(token => {
    const lastDashIndex = token.lastIndexOf('-');
    alphaColorNames.add(token.substring(0, lastDashIndex));
  });

  return alphaColorNames;
};

const processTokens = (
  obj: any,
  prefix: string = `--${CONFIG.prefix}`,
  path: string[] = []
): string[] => {
  if (path[0] === 'colors' && (path[1] === 'alpha' || path[1] === 'extended')) {
    return [];
  }

  return Object.entries(obj).flatMap(([key, value]) => {
    const propertyPath = `${prefix}-${key}`;
    const tokenPath = [...path, key];

    if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
      if (tokenPath[0] === 'typography' && key === 'text') {
        return processSemanticTextTokens(value, `--${CONFIG.prefix}-typography-text`, tokenPath);
      }
      return processTokens(value, propertyPath, tokenPath);
    }

    if (tokenPath[0] === 'colors') {
      return [`  ${propertyPath}: var(--${CONFIG.prefix}-theme-${value});`];
    }

    if (tokenPath[0] === 'typography' && tokenPath[1] === 'font-family') {
      return [`  ${propertyPath}: var(--${CONFIG.prefix}-theme-${value});`];
    }

    const resolvedValue = resolveTokenReferences(value as string);
    return [`  ${propertyPath}: ${resolvedValue};`];
  });
};

const processSemanticTextTokens = (obj: any, prefix: string, path: string[]): string[] => {
  return Object.entries(obj).flatMap(([key, value]) => {
    const propertyPath = `${prefix}-${key}`;
    const tokenPath = [...path, key];

    if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
      return processSemanticTextTokens(value, propertyPath, tokenPath);
    }

    return [`  ${propertyPath}: var(--${CONFIG.prefix}-typography-font-size-${value});`];
  });
};

const processAlphaTokens = (tokens: any): string[] => {
  if (!tokens.colors?.alpha || typeof tokens.colors.alpha !== 'object') {
    return [];
  }

  return Object.keys(tokens.colors.alpha).map(token => {
    const lastDashIndex = token.lastIndexOf('-');
    const colorName = token.substring(0, lastDashIndex);
    const opacity = token.substring(lastDashIndex + 1);
    const propertyPath = `--${CONFIG.prefix}-colors-alpha-${token}`;
    const alphaDecimal = alphaPercentToDecimal(opacity);

    return `  ${propertyPath}: rgba(var(--${CONFIG.prefix}-theme-${colorName}-rgb), ${alphaDecimal});`;
  });
};

const processExtendedColors = (tokens: any): string[] => {
  if (!tokens.colors?.extended || typeof tokens.colors.extended !== 'object') {
    return [];
  }

  return Object.entries(tokens.colors.extended).map(([colorName, colorValue]) => {
    const propertyPath = `--${CONFIG.prefix}-colors-extended-${colorName}`;
    return `  ${propertyPath}: ${colorValue};`;
  });
};

// =============================================
// CSS Generation
// =============================================
const generateGlobalCss = (tokens: any): string => {
  const standardTokens = processTokens(tokens);
  const alphaTokens = processAlphaTokens(tokens);
  const extendedColors = processExtendedColors(tokens);
  const allGridUtilities = generateAllGridUtilities(tokens);

  const cssLines = [
    ':root [data-theme] {',
    ...standardTokens,
    ...alphaTokens,
    ...extendedColors,
    '}',
    resetStyles,
    globalStyles,
    allGridUtilities,
  ];

  return cssLines.join('\n');
};

const generateThemeCss = async (
  theme: any,
  name: string,
  alphaColorNames: Set<string>,
  includeFonts: boolean = true
): Promise<string> => {
  let cssLines: string[] = [];

  if (includeFonts) {
    const fontNames = getThemeFontNames(theme);
    const fontCssPromises = fontNames.map(fontName => readFontCss(fontName, true));
    const fontCssContents = await Promise.all(fontCssPromises);
    const fontCss = fontCssContents.filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

    cssLines = [fontCss, ''];
  }

  cssLines.push(`:root [data-theme="${name}"] {`);

  Object.entries(theme).forEach(([property, value]) => {
    cssLines.push(`  --${CONFIG.prefix}-theme-${property}: ${value};`);

    if (alphaColorNames.has(property)) {
      try {
        const [r, g, b] = hexToRgb(value as string);
        cssLines.push(`  --${CONFIG.prefix}-theme-${property}-rgb: ${r}, ${g}, ${b};`);
      } catch (error) {
        console.warn(
          `Could not convert color '${property}: ${value}' to RGB components:`,
          error instanceof Error ? error.message : error
        );
      }
    }
  });

  cssLines.push('}\n');
  return cssLines.join('\n');
};

const generateAllCss = async (tokens: any, themes: any): Promise<string> => {
  const globalCss = generateGlobalCss(tokens);
  const alphaColorNames = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);

  const allFontNames = new Set<string>();
  Object.values(themes).forEach((theme: any) => {
    getThemeFontNames(theme).forEach(name => allFontNames.add(name));
  });

  const fontCssPromises = Array.from(allFontNames).map(fontName => readFontCss(fontName, true));
  const fontCssContents = await Promise.all(fontCssPromises);

  const fontCss = fontCssContents.filter(Boolean).join('\n\n');

  const themeCssPromises = Object.entries(themes).map(([themeName, themeValues]) =>
    generateThemeCss(themeValues, themeName, alphaColorNames, false)
  );
  const themeCssContents = await Promise.all(themeCssPromises);
  const themesCss = themeCssContents.join('\n');

  return `${fontCss}\n\n${globalCss}\n\n${themesCss}`;
};

// =============================================
// File Generation
// =============================================
const generateCssFile = async (
  cssGeneratorPromise: Promise<string>,
  fileName: string
): Promise<string> => {
  const css = await cssGeneratorPromise;
  const processedCss = processWithLightningCss(css, fileName);
  await writeFile(join(CONFIG.outputDir, fileName), processedCss);
  return fileName;
};

// =============================================
// Main Build Function
// =============================================
const generateCssFiles = async (): Promise<string[]> => {
  await ensureDir(CONFIG.outputDir);
  const alphaColorNames = getAlphaColorNames(tokens);

  const filePromises = [
    generateCssFile(Promise.resolve(generateGlobalCss(tokens)), 'global.css'),
    ...Object.entries(themes).map(([themeName, themeValues]) =>
      generateCssFile(
        generateThemeCss(themeValues, themeName, alphaColorNames, true),
        `theme-${themeName}.css`
      )
    ),
    generateCssFile(generateAllCss(tokens, themes), 'styles.css'),
  ];

  return Promise.all(filePromises);
};

export {
  ensureDir,
  writeFile,
  readFontCss,
  escapeRegExp,
  sanitizeFontName,
  getThemeFontNames,
  processWithLightningCss,
  getAlphaColorNames,
  processTokens,
  processSemanticTextTokens,
  processAlphaTokens,
  processExtendedColors,
  generateGlobalCss,
  generateThemeCss,
  generateAllCss,
  generateCssFile,
  generateCssFiles,
  CONFIG,
};



================================================
FILE: scripts/watch.js
================================================
import { join, dirname } from 'path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
import { exec } from 'child_process';
import fs from 'fs';
import chokidar from 'chokidar';

// Configuration to convert import.meta.url to __dirname in ESM
const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
const __dirname = dirname(__filename);

// Absolute path configuration
const rootDir = join(__dirname, '..');
const stylesDir = join(rootDir, 'src', 'styles');
const tokensDir = join(stylesDir, 'tokens');
const themesDir = join(stylesDir, 'themes');
const globalStylesDir = join(stylesDir, 'global');
const generateScriptPath = join(__dirname, 'cli', 'generate-css-cli.ts');

// Verification of important directories - this helps diagnose problems
console.log('\n=== DIRECTORY VERIFICATION ===');
[rootDir, stylesDir, tokensDir, themesDir, globalStylesDir, generateScriptPath].forEach(path => {
  const exists = fs.existsSync(path);
  const type = exists
    ? fs.lstatSync(path).isDirectory()
      ? 'directory'
      : 'file'
    : 'does not exist';
  console.log(`${path}: ${exists ? '✅' : '❌'} (${type})`);
});

// List all JS and TS files in the tokens directory
console.log('\nJS and TS files in tokens:');
if (fs.existsSync(tokensDir)) {
  const files = fs
    .readdirSync(tokensDir)
    .filter(file => file.endsWith('.js') || file.endsWith('.ts'))
    .map(file => join(tokensDir, file));

  files.forEach(file => console.log(` - ${file}`));

  // Configure specific files to watch
  const watchPaths = [
    ...files,
    ...fs
      .readdirSync(themesDir)
      .filter(file => file.endsWith('.js') || file.endsWith('.ts'))
      .map(file => join(themesDir, file)),
    ...fs
      .readdirSync(globalStylesDir)
      .filter(file => file.endsWith('.js') || file.endsWith('.ts'))
      .map(file => join(globalStylesDir, file)),
    generateScriptPath,
  ];

  console.log(`\n=== WATCHER CONFIGURATION ===`);
  console.log(`Watching ${watchPaths.length} specific files.`);

  // Function to run the generate.js script with better error handling
  const runGenerateScript = () => {
    console.log('\n🔄 Changes detected, running generate.ts...');

    exec(`npx vite-node ${generateScriptPath}`, (error, stdout, stderr) => {
      if (error) {
        console.error(`❌ Error executing generate.ts: ${error.message}`);
        return;
      }

      if (stderr) {
        console.error(`⚠️ Error in generate.ts: ${stderr}`);
      }

      console.log(`✅ CSS generated successfully:`);
      console.log(stdout);
    });
  };

  // Create a timer to handle multiple quick changes
  let debounceTimer;
  const debouncedGenerate = () => {
    clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    debounceTimer = setTimeout(runGenerateScript, 300);
  };

  // Configure watcher based on the specific file list
  const watcher = chokidar.watch(watchPaths, {
    persistent: true,
    ignoreInitial: true,
    awaitWriteFinish: {
      stabilityThreshold: 1000,
      pollInterval: 100,
    },
    // Add this to force polling instead of using file system events
    usePolling: true,
    interval: 1000, // Polling interval in ms
  });

  // Configure watcher events
  watcher
    .on('ready', () => {
      console.log('\n🔍 Watcher initialized and ready to detect changes');
      // Run generate.js at startup
      runGenerateScript();
    })
    .on('change', path => {
      console.log(`\n📝 File modified: ${path}`);
      debouncedGenerate();
    })
    .on('add', path => {
      console.log(`\n➕ New file detected: ${path}`);
      debouncedGenerate();
    })
    .on('unlink', path => {
      console.log(`\n❌ File deleted: ${path}`);
      debouncedGenerate();
    })
    .on('error', error => {
      console.error(`\n⛔ Watcher error: ${error}`);
    });

  // Handler to properly terminate the process
  process.on('SIGINT', () => {
    console.log('\nStopping the watcher...');
    watcher.close().then(() => {
      console.log('Watcher closed successfully.');
      process.exit(0);
    });
  });

  console.log('\n👀 Watching for changes in style files...');
} else {
  console.error('\n❌ Could not find the tokens directory or there are no JS files inside.');
}



================================================
FILE: scripts/cli/generate-css-cli.ts
================================================
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { generateCssFiles } from '../generate';

console.log('🚀 Starting CSS generation...');

generateCssFiles()
  .then(files => {
    console.log(`✅ CSS generation completed successfully for ${files.length} files!`);
    files.forEach(file => console.log(`   - Generated: ${file}`));
  })
  .catch(error => {
    console.error('❌ Error generating CSS files:', error);
    process.exit(1);
  });

FILE: src/components/atoms/avatar/col-avatar.styles.ts
================================================
import { css } from 'lit';
import { getThemeVar } from '@/styles/utils';

export const ColAvatarStyles = css`
  .avatar-container {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: ${getThemeVar('spacing.size.xs')};
    font-family: ${getThemeVar('typography.font-family.primary')};
    color: ${getThemeVar('colors.text.default')};
  }

  .avatar-circle {
    width: 24px;
    height: 24px;
    box-sizing: border-box;
    padding: ${getThemeVar('spacing.size.2xs')};
    border-radius: 50%;
    background-color: ${getThemeVar('colors.ui.success')};
    color: ${getThemeVar('colors.text.inverse')};
    text-transform: uppercase;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: ${getThemeVar('typography.text.supporting.label-xs')};
    line-height: 16px;
  }

  .avatar-name {
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: ${getThemeVar('typography.text.heading.semibold-s')};
    line-height: 20px;
  }
`;
