import { expect, fixture, html, elementUpdated } from '@open-wc/testing';
import { TextInputBase } from './text-input-base';

class TestTextInputComponent extends TextInputBase {
  protected _getInputType(): string {
    return 'text';
  }

  validate(_value: unknown): boolean {
    return true;
  }

  public updateDirtyState() {
    this._updateDirtyState();
  }

  public countCharacters(e: globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement }) {
    this._countCharacters(e);
  }

  public handleInput(e: globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement }) {
    this._handleInput(e);
  }

  public handleClear() {
    this._handleClear();
  }

  public getSupportsClearing() {
    return this._supportsClearing;
  }

  public getHasValue() {
    return this._hasValue;
  }

  public getCountValue() {
    return this.countValue;
  }

  public setCountValue(value: number) {
    this.countValue = value;
  }

  public getInputId() {
    return this.inputId;
  }

  public handleChange() {
    this._handleChange();
  }
}

describe('TextInputBase', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    if (!customElements.get('test-text-input-component')) {
      customElements.define('test-text-input-component', TestTextInputComponent);
    }
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    document.querySelectorAll('test-text-input-component').forEach(el => el.remove());
  });

  describe('Properties', () => {
    it('sets value property correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component value="test value"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.value).to.equal('test value');
    });

    it('sets placeholder property correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component placeholder="Enter text here"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.placeholder).to.equal('Enter text here');
    });

    it('sets readOnly property correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component readonly></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.readOnly).to.be.true;
    });

    it('sets pattern property correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component pattern="[A-Za-z]+"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.pattern).to.equal('[A-Za-z]+');
    });

    it('sets minLength property correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component min-length="5"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.minLength).to.equal(5);
    });
  });

  describe('Rendering', () => {
    it('renders input with correct type', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`);
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.type).to.equal('text');
    });

    it('renders input with placeholder', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component placeholder="Enter text"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.placeholder).to.equal('Enter text');
    });

    it('renders input with value', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component value="initial value"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.value).to.equal('initial value');
    });

    it('renders input with readonly attribute when readOnly is true', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component readonly></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.readOnly).to.be.true;
    });

    it('renders input with pattern attribute', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component pattern="[A-Za-z]+"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.pattern).to.equal('[A-Za-z]+');
    });

    it('renders input with minlength attribute', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component min-length="5"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;
      expect(input.minLength).to.equal(5);
    });

    it('renders input with common attributes from InputFormComponent', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component
          label="Test Label"
          required
          disabled
          helper="Helper text"
          input-mode="email"
        ></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      expect(input.required).to.be.true;
      expect(input.disabled).to.be.true;
      expect(input.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')).to.include('label');
      expect(input.getAttribute('aria-describedby')).to.include('helper');
      expect(input.inputMode).to.equal('email');
    });
  });

  describe('Input handling', () => {
    it('updates value on input event', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'new value';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);

      expect(el.value).to.equal('new value');
    });

    it('updates character count on input', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component char-count="10"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'hello';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);

      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(5);
    });

    it('enforces character count limit', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component char-count="5"></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'hello world';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);

      expect(el.value).to.equal('hello');
      expect(input.value).to.equal('hello');
      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(5);
    });

    it('syncs form value on input', async () => {
      const form = await fixture(
        html`<form>
          <test-text-input-component name="test-input"></test-text-input-component>
        </form>`
      );
      const el = form.querySelector('test-text-input-component') as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'form value';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);
      await elementUpdated(form);

      const formData = new FormData(form as HTMLFormElement);
      expect(formData.get('test-input')).to.equal('form value');
    });
  });

  describe('Dirty state tracking', () => {
    it('updates dirty state when value changes', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;

      (el as any).initialValue = '';
      expect(el.dirty).to.be.false;

      el.value = 'new value';
      el.updateDirtyState();

      expect(el.dirty).to.be.true;
    });

    it('resets dirty state when value returns to initial', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;

      (el as any).initialValue = 'initial';
      el.value = 'initial';
      el.updateDirtyState();

      expect(el.dirty).to.be.false;
    });
  });

  describe('Clear functionality', () => {
    it('supports clearing by default', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      expect(el.getSupportsClearing()).to.be.true;
    });

    it('has value when value is not empty', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;

      expect(el.getHasValue()).to.be.false;

      el.value = 'test';
      expect(el.getHasValue()).to.be.true;
    });

    it('shows clear button when has value', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;

      let clearButton = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('.icon-close-container');
      expect(clearButton).to.be.null;

      el.value = 'test';
      await elementUpdated(el);

      clearButton = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('.icon-close-container');
      expect(clearButton).to.exist;
      expect(clearButton?.getAttribute('aria-label')).to.equal('Clear input');
    });

    it('clears value when clear button is clicked', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      el.value = 'test value';
      await elementUpdated(el);

      const clearButton = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector(
        '.icon-close-container'
      ) as HTMLButtonElement;
      clearButton.click();
      await elementUpdated(el);

      expect(el.value).to.equal('');
      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(0);
    });

    it('dispatches events when clearing', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      el.value = 'test value';
      await elementUpdated(el);

      let inputFired = false;
      let changeFired = false;

      el.addEventListener('input', () => {
        inputFired = true;
      });

      el.addEventListener('change', () => {
        changeFired = true;
      });

      el.handleClear();

      expect(inputFired).to.be.true;
      expect(changeFired).to.be.true;
    });

    it('updates dirty state when clearing', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      (el as any).initialValue = 'initial';
      el.value = 'modified';
      el.updateDirtyState();
      expect(el.dirty).to.be.true;

      el.handleClear();
      expect(el.dirty).to.be.true;
    });
  });

  describe('Character counting', () => {
    it('updates character count correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const mockEvent = {
        target: { value: 'hello world' },
      } as globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement };

      el.countCharacters(mockEvent);
      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(11);
    });

    it('handles empty string in character counting', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const mockEvent = {
        target: { value: '' },
      } as globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement };

      el.countCharacters(mockEvent);
      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(0);
    });

    it('displays character count when charCount is set', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component char-count="10"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const charCountElement = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('.input-char-count');
      expect(charCountElement).to.exist;
      expect(charCountElement?.textContent?.trim()).to.equal('0/10');
    });

    it('updates character count display on input', async () => {
      const el = await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component char-count="10"></test-text-input-component>`
      );
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'hello';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);

      const charCountElement = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('.input-char-count');
      expect(charCountElement?.textContent?.trim()).to.equal('5/10');
    });
  });

  describe('Form integration', () => {
    it('participates in form submission', async () => {
      const form = await fixture(
        html`<form>
          <test-text-input-component name="username"></test-text-input-component>
        </form>`
      );
      const el = form.querySelector('test-text-input-component') as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.value = 'testuser';
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true, composed: true }));
      await elementUpdated(el);
      await elementUpdated(form);

      const formData = new FormData(form as HTMLFormElement);
      expect(formData.get('username')).to.equal('testuser');
    });

    it('handles form reset', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      el.value = 'test value';
      el.setCountValue(5);
      el.touched = true;
      el.dirty = true;
      el.valid = false;

      el._handleFormReset();

      expect(el.value).to.equal('');
      expect(el.getCountValue()).to.equal(0);
      expect(el.touched).to.be.false;
      expect(el.dirty).to.be.false;
      expect(el.valid).to.be.true;
    });
  });

  describe('Accessibility', () => {
    it('has proper ARIA attributes for clear button', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      el.value = 'test';
      await elementUpdated(el);

      const clearButton = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector(
        '.icon-close-container'
      ) as HTMLButtonElement;
      expect(clearButton.getAttribute('aria-label')).to.equal('Clear input');
      expect(clearButton.getAttribute('aria-controls')).to.equal(el.getInputId());
      expect(clearButton.getAttribute('aria-describedby')).to.equal(el.getInputId());
    });

    it('associates clear button with input', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      el.value = 'test';
      await elementUpdated(el);

      const clearButton = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector(
        '.icon-close-container'
      ) as HTMLButtonElement;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      expect(clearButton.getAttribute('aria-controls')).to.equal(input.id);
    });
  });

  describe('Event handling', () => {
    it('handles focus events correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      expect(el.focused).to.be.false;
      expect(el.touched).to.be.false;

      input.focus();
      await elementUpdated(el);

      expect(el.focused).to.be.true;
      expect(el.touched).to.be.true;
    });

    it('handles blur events correctly', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      const input = el.shadowRoot?.querySelector('input') as HTMLInputElement;

      input.focus();
      await elementUpdated(el);
      expect(el.focused).to.be.true;

      input.blur();
      await elementUpdated(el);
      expect(el.focused).to.be.false;
    });

    it('dispatches change events', async () => {
      const el = (await fixture(
        html`<test-text-input-component></test-text-input-component>`
      )) as TestTextInputComponent;
      let changeFired = false;
      let changeValue = '';

      el.addEventListener('change', (e: any) => {
        changeFired = true;
        changeValue = e.detail.value;
      });

      el.value = 'new value';
      el.handleChange();

      expect(changeFired).to.be.true;
      expect(changeValue).to.equal('new value');
    });
  });
});



================================================
FILE: src/config/components/input-form-component/text-input-base.ts
================================================
import { html, nothing } from 'lit';
import { property, query } from 'lit/decorators.js';
import { InputFormComponent } from './input-form-component';

/**
 * Base component for text-based input elements
 *
 * Extends InputFormComponent to provide common text input functionality:
 * - Value management and synchronization
 * - Placeholder text support
 * - Read-only state
 * - Input validation patterns
 * - Minimum length constraints
 * - Input mode specification
 * - Character counting
 * - Clear functionality
 * - Dirty state tracking
 *
 * Subclasses must implement:
 * - _getInputType(): Returns the HTML input type attribute
 *
 */
export abstract class TextInputBase extends InputFormComponent {
  /**
   * Placeholder text displayed when the input is empty
   */
  @property({ type: String })
  placeholder = '';

  /**
   * Whether the input is read-only and cannot be modified
   */
  @property({ type: Boolean })
  readOnly = false;

  /**
   * HTML pattern attribute for input validation
   * Uses regex pattern to validate input format
   */
  @property({ type: String })
  pattern?: string;

  /**
   * Minimum number of characters required for the input
   * Maps to the 'min-length' attribute
   */
  @property({ type: Number, attribute: 'min-length' })
  minLength?: number;

  /**
   * The input element
   */
  @query('input')
  private inputElement!: HTMLInputElement;

  /**
   * Whether the component supports clearing functionality
   *
   * @returns boolean - Always true for text inputs
   *
   * Text inputs support clearing by default, so this returns true.
   */
  protected override get _supportsClearing(): boolean {
    return true;
  }

  /**
   * Whether the component currently has a value
   *
   * @returns boolean - True if the value is not empty
   *
   * Checks if the value property contains any text.
   */
  protected override get _hasValue(): boolean {
    return !!this.value;
  }

  /**
   * Enhanced clear handler that triggers validation after clearing
   *
   * Clears the input value and resets related state:
   * - Sets value to empty string
   * - Resets character count to 0
   * - Updates dirty state
   * - Dispatches input and change events
   * - Syncs form value
   * - Triggers validation after clearing
   */
  protected override _handleClear(): void {
    this.value = '';
    this.countValue = 0;
    this._updateDirtyState();
    this._dispatchInput(this.value);
    this._syncFormValue();
    this._handleChange();

    requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      this._updateValidState();
    });
  }

  /**
   * Updates the dirty state based on value changes
   *
   * Compares the current value with the initial value to determine
   * if the input has been modified by the user.
   */
  protected _updateDirtyState() {
    this.dirty = this.value !== this.initialValue;
  }

  /**
   * Updates the character count based on input value
   *
   * @param e - The input event containing the target element
   *
   * Extracts the value from the input event and updates the
   * countValue state with the current character count.
   */
  protected _countCharacters(e: globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement }) {
    let { value } = e.target;
    this.countValue = value.length;
  }

  /**
   * Enhanced input handler that updates padding when clear button visibility changes
   * and triggers validation based on validation timing settings
   */
  protected override _handleInput(
    e: globalThis.Event & { target: globalThis.HTMLInputElement }
  ): void {
    const oldValue = this.value;
    let inputValue = e.target.value;

    if (typeof this.charCount === 'number' && inputValue.length > this.charCount) {
      inputValue = inputValue.slice(0, this.charCount);
      e.target.value = inputValue;
    }

    this.value = inputValue;
    this._countCharacters(e);
    this._updateDirtyState();
    this._dispatchInput(this.value);
    this._syncFormValue();

    if (this._shouldValidate('input')) {
      this._updateValidState();
    }

    if ((!oldValue && this.value) || (oldValue && !this.value)) {
      this._updateInputPadding();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Renders the text input element
   *
   * @returns TemplateResult - The rendered input element
   *
   * Creates an HTML input element with:
   * - Common attributes from InputFormComponent
   * - Text-specific attributes (placeholder, pattern, minLength, inputMode)
   * - Event handlers for focus, blur, change, and input events
   * - Proper ARIA attributes for accessibility
   * - Error styling when in error state
   */
  protected override renderInput() {
    const attrs = this._getCommonInputAttributes();
    const events = this._getCommonInputEventHandlers();

    return html`
      <input
        class=${attrs.errorClass}
        type="${this._getInputType()}"
        name="${attrs.name}"
        id="${attrs.id}"
        aria-labelledby=${attrs.ariaLabelledby}
        aria-describedby=${attrs.ariaDescribedby}
        aria-invalid=${attrs.ariaInvalid}
        aria-required=${attrs.ariaRequired}
        ?required=${attrs.required}
        placeholder="${this.placeholder}"
        .value="${this.value}"
        ?disabled=${attrs.disabled}
        ?readonly=${this.readOnly}
        pattern="${this.pattern || nothing}"
        minlength="${this.minLength || nothing}"
        inputmode="${this.inputMode || nothing}"
        @focus=${events.onFocus}
        @blur=${this._handleBlur}
        @change=${events.onChange}
        @input=${this._handleInput}
        @invalid=${this._handleInvalid}
      />
    `;
  }

  /**
   * Updates the validation state of the component
   *
   * Validates the current value using the component's validate() method
   * and updates the component's validation state properties (valid, error, errorMessage).
   * Also integrates with ElementInternals for native form validation.
   */
  protected _updateValidState(): void {
    if (!this.inputElement) return;

    this.valid = this.validate(this.value);

    if (!this.valid) {
      const browserMessage = this.inputElement.validationMessage;
      if (!this.errorMessage && browserMessage) {
        this.errorMessage = browserMessage;
      }
      this.error = true;
      this._internals.setValidity(
        { customError: true },
        this.errorMessage || browserMessage || 'Invalid value',
        this.inputElement
      );
    } else {
      this.error = false;
      this._internals.setValidity({}, '');
    }
    this._dispatchValidationState(this.inputElement);
  }

  /**
   * Dispatches validation state change event
   *
   * @param input - The input element to extract validation details from
   *
   * Emits a 'validation-change' custom event with comprehensive validation
   * information including validity state, validation message, and form states.
   */
  protected _dispatchValidationState(input: HTMLInputElement): void {
    this.dispatchEvent(
      new CustomEvent('validation-change', {
        detail: {
          valid: this.valid,
          validationMessage: input.validationMessage,
          validity: {
            badInput: input.validity.badInput,
            patternMismatch: input.validity.patternMismatch,
            rangeOverflow: input.validity.rangeOverflow,
            rangeUnderflow: input.validity.rangeUnderflow,
            stepMismatch: input.validity.stepMismatch,
            tooLong: input.validity.tooLong,
            tooShort: input.validity.tooShort,
            typeMismatch: input.validity.typeMismatch,
            valueMissing: input.validity.valueMissing,
            valid: input.validity.valid,
          },
          value: this.value,
          touched: this.touched,
          dirty: this.dirty,
        },
        bubbles: true,
        composed: true,
      })
    );
  }

  /**
   * Handles the native HTML invalid event
   *
   * @param e - The invalid event from the input element
   *
   * Updates component validation state and dispatches a custom invalid event
   * with detailed validation information. Prevents the default browser
   * validation popup from appearing.
   */
  protected _handleInvalid = (e: Event): void => {
    e.preventDefault();
    const input = e.target as HTMLInputElement;

    this.valid = false;
    this.error = true;

    const validationMessage = input.validationMessage;

    if (!this.errorMessage && validationMessage) {
      this.errorMessage = validationMessage;
    }

    this.dispatchEvent(
      new CustomEvent('invalid', {
        detail: {
          validationMessage,
          validity: {
            badInput: input.validity.badInput,
            patternMismatch: input.validity.patternMismatch,
            rangeOverflow: input.validity.rangeOverflow,
            rangeUnderflow: input.validity.rangeUnderflow,
            stepMismatch: input.validity.stepMismatch,
            tooLong: input.validity.tooLong,
            tooShort: input.validity.tooShort,
            typeMismatch: input.validity.typeMismatch,
            valueMissing: input.validity.valueMissing,
            valid: input.validity.valid,
          },
          value: this.value,
          touched: this.touched,
          dirty: this.dirty,
          focused: this.focused,
        },
        bubbles: true,
        composed: true,
      })
    );
  };

  /**
   * Enhanced blur handler that triggers validation based on validation timing settings
   *
   * @param _e - The blur event (unused but kept for signature compatibility)
   */
  protected _handleBlur = (_e: FocusEvent): void => {
    this.focused = false;

    if (this._shouldValidate('blur')) {
      this._updateValidState();
    }

    this._syncFormValue();
    this.dispatchEvent(
      new Event('blur', {
        bubbles: true,
        composed: true,
      })
    );
  };

  /**
   * Abstract method that subclasses must implement for validation logic
   *
   * @param value - The value to validate
   * @returns boolean - True if the value is valid, false otherwise
   *
   * Each text input component must implement its own validation rules.
   * This method is called by _updateValidState() to determine validity.
   */
  public abstract validate(value: unknown): boolean;

  /**
   * Abstract method that subclasses must implement
   *
   * @returns string - The HTML input type attribute value
   *
   * This method should return the appropriate input type for the
   * specific text input component:
   * - 'text' for regular text inputs
   * - 'email' for email inputs
   * - 'password' for password inputs
   * - 'search' for search inputs
   * - 'tel' for telephone inputs
   * - 'url' for URL inputs
   * - etc.
   */
  protected abstract _getInputType(): string;
}



================================================
FILE: src/config/components/selectable-form-component/selectable-form-component.ts
================================================
import { property } from 'lit/decorators.js';
import { FormComponent } from '@/config/components/form-component/form-component';

/**
 * Form component that can be selected/checked with mouse and keyboard support
 *
 * This component serves as a base for components like:
 * - Custom checkboxes
 * - Custom radio buttons
 * - Toggle switches
 * - Selectable list items
 */
export class SelectableFormComponent extends FormComponent {
  /**
   * Whether the component is currently checked/selected
   */
  @property({ type: Boolean, reflect: true }) checked = false;

  /**
   * Whether the component is disabled and cannot be interacted with
   */
  @property({ type: Boolean, reflect: true }) disabled = false;

  /**
   * Unique identifier for this component instance
   *
   * Used for:
   * - Accessibility labeling (id/for relationships)
   * - Form field identification
   * - Testing and debugging
   * - Ensuring uniqueness across multiple instances
   */
  protected _id = `${this.constructor.name.toLowerCase()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 11)}`;

  /**
   * Lifecycle method called when component is added to the DOM
   *
   * Sets up event listeners for user interactions:
   * - mousedown: Adds visual active state
   * - mouseup: Removes visual active state
   * - keydown: Handles keyboard navigation/selection
   *
   * Calls super.connectedCallback() to maintain parent functionality.
   */
  connectedCallback() {
    super.connectedCallback();
    this.addEventListener('mousedown', this._handleMouseDown);
    this.addEventListener('mouseup', this._handleMouseUp);
    this.addEventListener('keydown', this._handleKeyDown);
  }

  /**
   * Lifecycle method called when component is removed from the DOM
   *
   * Cleans up event listeners to prevent memory leaks:
   * - Removes mousedown listener
   * - Removes mouseup listener
   * - Removes keydown listener
   *
   * Calls super.disconnectedCallback() to maintain parent cleanup.
   */
  disconnectedCallback() {
    super.disconnectedCallback();
    this.removeEventListener('mousedown', this._handleMouseDown);
    this.removeEventListener('mouseup', this._handleMouseUp);
    this.removeEventListener('keydown', this._handleKeyDown);
  }

  /**
   * Handles mouse button press events
   *
   * Provides visual feedback during mouse interactions by:
   * - Adding 'data-active' attribute for CSS styling
   * - Only activating if component is not disabled
   * - Creating pressed/active visual state
   */
  private _handleMouseDown = () => {
    if (!this.disabled) {
      this.setAttribute('data-active', '');
    }
  };

  /**
   * Handles mouse button release events
   *
   * Removes the visual active state by:
   * - Removing 'data-active' attribute
   * - Returning component to normal visual state
   *
   * This completes the visual feedback cycle started by _handleMouseDown.
   */
  private _handleMouseUp = () => {
    this.removeAttribute('data-active');
  };

  /**
   * Handles keyboard navigation and selection events
   *
   * @param e - The keyboard event
   *
   * Provides keyboard accessibility by:
   * - Preventing default behavior for handled keys
   * - Stopping event propagation when disabled
   * - Supporting Space and Enter keys for selection
   * - Following ARIA keyboard interaction patterns
   *
   * When disabled:
   * - Prevents all keyboard interaction
   * - Stops event bubbling to parent elements
   *
   * For Space/Enter keys:
   * - Prevents default scrolling/form submission
   * - Triggers the same selection logic as mouse clicks
   */
  private _handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
    if (this.disabled) {
      e.preventDefault();
      e.stopPropagation();
      return;
    }

    if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Space' || e.key === 'Enter') {
      e.preventDefault();
      this._handleClick();
    }
  };

  /**
   * Handles click/selection events from mouse or keyboard
   *
   * @param e - Optional event object (from mouse clicks)
   *
   * Central handler for all selection attempts:
   * - Validates that component is not disabled
   * - Prevents default behavior and event bubbling when disabled
   * - Delegates actual selection logic to _performSelection()
   *
   * This method serves as a gateway that:
   * - Enforces disabled state consistently
   * - Provides a single point for selection validation
   * - Allows subclasses to focus on selection logic rather than validation
   */
  protected _handleClick(e?: Event) {
    if (this.disabled) {
      if (e) {
        e.preventDefault();
        e.stopPropagation();
      }
      return;
    }

    this._performSelection();
  }

  /**
   * Performs the actual selection/toggle logic - must be implemented by subclasses
   *
   * @throws Error if not implemented by subclass
   */
  protected _performSelection(): void {
    // Override in subclasses
    throw new Error('_performSelection must be implemented by subclass');
  }
}

FILE: src/config/components/themed-component/themed-component.test.ts
================================================
import { html, fixture, expect, elementUpdated } from '@open-wc/testing';
import { ThemedComponent } from './themed-component';

class TestThemedComponent extends ThemedComponent {
  static tagName = 'test-themed-component';
  tokensName = 'TEST-THEMED-COMPONENT';

  render() {
    return html`<div>Test Themed Component</div>`;
  }
}

// Custom component for testing custom tokensName
class CustomTokensComponent extends ThemedComponent {
  static tagName = 'custom-tokens-component';
  tokensName = 'CUSTOM-TOKENS';

  render() {
    return html`<div>Custom Tokens Component</div>`;
  }
}

describe('ThemedComponent', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    if (!customElements.get('test-themed-component')) {
      customElements.define('test-themed-component', TestThemedComponent);
    }
    if (!customElements.get('custom-tokens-component')) {
      customElements.define('custom-tokens-component', CustomTokensComponent);
    }
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    document
      .querySelectorAll('test-themed-component, custom-tokens-component')
      .forEach(el => el.remove());
  });

  describe('Tokens functionality', () => {
    it('should update CSS custom properties when tokens are set', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: '#ff0000',
          secondary: '#00ff00',
        },
        spacing: {
          small: '4px',
        },
      };

      element.tokens = tokens;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('#ff0000');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-secondary')).to.equal('#00ff00');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-small')).to.equal('4px');
    });

    it('should clear custom properties when tokens are set to undefined', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: '#ff0000',
        },
      };

      element.tokens = tokens;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('#ff0000');

      element.tokens = undefined;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.cssText).to.equal('');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('');
    });

    it('should handle nested token objects', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const tokens = {
        colors: {
          brand: {
            primary: '#ff0000',
            secondary: '#00ff00',
          },
          text: {
            heading: '#333333',
            body: '#666666',
          },
        },
      };

      element.tokens = tokens;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-brand-primary')).to.equal('#ff0000');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-brand-secondary')).to.equal('#00ff00');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-text-heading')).to.equal('#333333');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-text-body')).to.equal('#666666');
    });

    it('should handle empty tokens object', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      element.tokens = {};
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.cssText).to.equal('');
    });

    it('should override existing custom properties', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      element.tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: '#ff0000',
        },
      };
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('#ff0000');

      element.tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: '#0000ff',
        },
        spacing: {
          large: '16px',
        },
      };
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('#0000ff');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-large')).to.equal('16px');
    });
  });

  describe('Slot detection', () => {
    it('should detect if a slot is present', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(html`
        <test-themed-component>
          <div slot="test-slot">Slotted content</div>
          <div>Default content</div>
        </test-themed-component>
      `);

      expect(element.hasSlotted('test-slot')).to.be.true;
      expect(element.hasSlotted('non-existent-slot')).to.be.false;
    });

    it('should detect multiple slots', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(html`
        <test-themed-component>
          <div slot="header">Header content</div>
          <div slot="footer">Footer content</div>
          <div>Default content</div>
        </test-themed-component>
      `);

      expect(element.hasSlotted('header')).to.be.true;
      expect(element.hasSlotted('footer')).to.be.true;
      expect(element.hasSlotted('sidebar')).to.be.false;
    });

    it('should return false for empty slot name', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(html`
        <test-themed-component>
          <div slot="">Empty slot name</div>
        </test-themed-component>
      `);

      expect(element.hasSlotted('')).to.be.true;
      expect(element.hasSlotted('non-existent')).to.be.false;
    });

    it('should detect slots added dynamically', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      expect(element.hasSlotted('dynamic-slot')).to.be.false;

      const dynamicElement = document.createElement('div');
      dynamicElement.setAttribute('slot', 'dynamic-slot');
      dynamicElement.textContent = 'Dynamic content';
      element.appendChild(dynamicElement);

      expect(element.hasSlotted('dynamic-slot')).to.be.true;
    });
  });

  describe('Component rendering', () => {
    it('should render the test component content', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const content = element.shadowRoot?.querySelector('div');
      expect(content).to.exist;
      expect(content?.textContent).to.equal('Test Themed Component');
    });

    it('should render the custom tokens component content', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<CustomTokensComponent>(
        html`<custom-tokens-component></custom-tokens-component>`
      );

      const content = element.shadowRoot?.querySelector('div');
      expect(content).to.exist;
      expect(content?.textContent).to.equal('Custom Tokens Component');
    });
  });

  describe('Property defaults', () => {
    it('should have correct default property values', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      expect(element.tokens).to.be.undefined;
      expect(element.tokensName).to.equal('TEST-THEMED-COMPONENT');
    });

    it('should have correct tagName property', async () => {
      expect(TestThemedComponent.tagName).to.equal('test-themed-component');
      expect(CustomTokensComponent.tagName).to.equal('custom-tokens-component');
    });
  });

  describe('Combined theme and tokens', () => {
    it('should handle theme and tokens together', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      element.dataset.theme = 'dark' as any;
      element.tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: '#ff0000',
        },
      };
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.dataset.theme).to.equal('dark');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('#ff0000');
    });

    it('should update both theme and tokens independently', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component theme="light"></test-themed-component>`
      );
      element.dataset.theme = 'light' as any;
      element.tokens = {
        spacing: {
          large: '20px',
        },
      };
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.dataset.theme).to.equal('light');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-large')).to.equal('20px');

      element.dataset.theme = 'dark' as any;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.dataset.theme).to.equal('dark');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-large')).to.equal('20px');
    });
  });

  describe('Edge cases', () => {
    it('should handle null values in tokens', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const tokens = {
        colors: {
          primary: null,
          secondary: '#00ff00',
        },
      };

      element.tokens = tokens as any;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-primary')).to.equal('');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-colors-secondary')).to.equal('#00ff00');
    });

    it('should handle numeric values in tokens', async () => {
      const element = await fixture<TestThemedComponent>(
        html`<test-themed-component></test-themed-component>`
      );

      const tokens = {
        spacing: {
          size: 16,
          ratio: 1.5,
        },
      };

      element.tokens = tokens as any;
      await elementUpdated(element);

      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-size')).to.equal('16');
      expect(element.style.getPropertyValue('--col-spacing-ratio')).to.equal('1.5');
    });
  });
});

FILE: src/config/components/themed-component/themed-component.ts
================================================
import { LitElement } from 'lit';
import { property } from 'lit/decorators.js';
import { TAG_PREFIX } from '@/config/constants';
import { ThemeTokens } from '@/styles/types';

/**
 * Abstract base component that provides theming functionality for all custom elements
 *
 * This component serves as the foundation for all themed UI components by providing:
 * - Theme mode switching (telesign/massive) Another if registered
 * - Custom design token override capabilities
 * - CSS custom property management
 * - Slot content detection utilities
 */
export abstract class ThemedComponent extends LitElement {
  /**
   * Static property that must be defined by subclasses
   */
  static readonly tagName: string;

  /**
   * Custom design tokens that override default theme values
   *
   * Allows fine-grained customization of component appearance by overriding
   * specific design tokens. The tokens object structure should match the
   * theme expected token hierarchy and will be flattened into
   * CSS custom properties automatically.
   */
  @property({ type: Object })
  tokens?: Partial<ThemeTokens>;

  /**
   * Lifecycle method called after component properties have been updated
   *
   * @param changedProperties - Map of properties that have changed
   *
   * Handles theme and token updates by:
   * - Setting data-theme attribute when theme property changes
   * - Regenerating CSS custom properties when tokens change
   * - Maintaining proper cleanup of unused attributes/styles
   *
   * Calls super.updated() to maintain LitElement's update lifecycle.
   */
  protected updated(changedProperties: Map<string, unknown>) {
    super.updated(changedProperties);

    if (changedProperties.has('tokens')) {
      this.updateTokens();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Updates the component's CSS custom properties based on current tokens
   *
   * This method:
   * - Clears all existing inline styles to prevent stale custom properties
   * - Converts the nested tokens object into flat CSS custom properties
   * - Applies the custom properties to the component's style attribute
   *
   * The conversion process flattens nested objects into kebab-case CSS
   * custom property names with the appropriate prefix.
   */
  private updateTokens() {
    // Clear existing custom properties
    this.style.cssText = '';

    if (!this.tokens) return;

    // Convert nested tokens object to CSS custom properties
    const customProperties = this.flattenTokens(this.tokens);

    // Apply custom properties to the element
    Object.entries(customProperties).forEach(([property, value]) => {
      this.style.setProperty(property, value);
    });
  }

  /**
   * Recursively flattens a nested tokens object into CSS custom properties
   *
   * @param tokens - The nested tokens object to flatten
   * @param prefix - The CSS custom property prefix (defaults to --{TAG_PREFIX})
   * @returns Record of flattened CSS custom property names and their values
   *
   * Conversion process:
   * - Nested objects become kebab-case paths: { color: { primary: 'red' } } → '--prefix-color-primary'
   * - Primitive values are preserved as strings
   * - Object keys are converted to lowercase and connected with hyphens
   */
  private flattenTokens(tokens: object, prefix = `--${TAG_PREFIX}`): Record<string, string> {
    const result: Record<string, string> = {};

    const processObject = (obj: any, currentPath: string) => {
      Object.entries(obj).forEach(([key, value]) => {
        const newPath = `${currentPath}-${key}`;

        if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
          processObject(value, newPath);
        } else {
          result[newPath] = value as string;
        }
      });
    };

    processObject(tokens, prefix);
    return result;
  }

  /**
   * Utility method to check if a specific slot has content
   *
   * @param slotName - The name of the slot to check
   * @returns true if the slot contains slotted content, false otherwise
   */
  hasSlotted(slotName: string) {
    const slot = this.querySelector(`[slot="${slotName}"]`);
    return slot !== null;
  }
}

FILE: src/config/contexts/site-menu-context.ts
================================================
import { createContext } from '@lit/context';
import { SITE_MENU_COLOR_VARIANTS } from '@/types';

/**
 * Site Menu Context Value Interface
 *
 * Provides centralized state management and action handlers for the Site Menu organism.
 */
export interface SiteMenuContextValue {
  /**
   * Current color variant applied to all site menu components
   */
  color: SITE_MENU_COLOR_VARIANTS;

  /**
   * ID of the currently selected action button (undefined if none selected)
   */
  selectedActionId?: string;

  /**
   * Whether the submenu panel is currently visible
   */
  open: boolean;

  /**
   * ID of the currently open subcategory (undefined if none open)
   * Enforces single-open accordion behavior
   */
  openSubcategoryId?: string;

  /**
   * Selects an action button and shows the submenu
   * @param actionId - The ID of the action to select
   */
  selectAction: (actionId: string) => void;

  /**
   * Deselects all action buttons and hides the submenu
   */
  deselectAllActions: () => void;

  /**
   * Toggles submenu visibility
   * @param visible - Optional explicit visibility state
   */
  toggleSubmenu: (visible?: boolean) => void;

  /**
   * Closes the submenu and clears action selection
   */
  closeSubmenu: () => void;

  /**
   * Toggles a subcategory open/close with single-open enforcement
   * If subcategory is already open, closes it
   * If another subcategory is open, closes it and opens the new one
   * If no subcategory is open, opens the specified one
   * @param subcategoryId - The ID of the subcategory to toggle
   */
  toggleSubcategory: (subcategoryId: string) => void;

  /**
   * Explicitly opens a subcategory, closing any other open subcategory
   * @param subcategoryId - The ID of the subcategory to open
   */
  openSubcategory: (subcategoryId: string) => void;

  /**
   * Closes all subcategories
   */
  closeAllSubcategories: () => void;

  /**
   * Reference to the currently focused action button element
   * Used for focus management when closing submenu
   */
  focusedActionElement?: HTMLElement;

  /**
   * Sets the focused action element reference
   * Called by action buttons when they receive focus
   * @param element - The action button element that should receive focus when submenu closes
   */
  setFocusedActionElement: (element: HTMLElement) => void;

  /**
   * Returns focus to the last focused action element
   * Used when closing submenu to restore focus to the triggering button
   */
  restoreFocus: () => void;
}

/**
 * Site Menu Context
 *
 * Context instance for the Site Menu organism system.
 * Components within the Site Menu tree can consume this context to:
 * - Access current state (color, selection, visibility)
 * - Trigger state changes through action handlers
 * - React to state updates automatically
 */
export const siteMenuContext = createContext<SiteMenuContextValue>('site-menu');

FILE: src/config/controllers/form-reset.controller.ts
================================================
import { ReactiveController, ReactiveControllerHost } from 'lit';

/**
 * Interface that form components must implement to use FormResetController
 */
export interface FormResetCapable extends ReactiveControllerHost {
  /**
   * Method called when the form containing this component is reset
   * Components must implement this to define their reset behavior
   */
  _handleFormReset(): void;
}

/**
 * A controller that adds form reset functionality to form components
 *
 * This controller ensures that only one reset event listener is added per form,
 * regardless of how many form components are in the form. It uses a static
 * registry to track forms and their associated components.
 *
 * Usage:
 * ```typescript
 * export class MyFormComponent extends FormComponent implements FormResetCapable {
 *   private formResetController = new FormResetController(this);
 *
 *   _handleFormReset(): void {
 *     // Reset component state here
 *     this.value = this.initialValue;
 *     this._syncFormValue();
 *   }
 * }
 * ```
 */
export class FormResetController implements ReactiveController {
  /**
   * Static registry mapping forms to their form reset managers
   * Using WeakMap ensures automatic cleanup when forms are garbage collected
   */
  private static formManagers = new WeakMap<HTMLFormElement, FormResetManager>();

  /**
   * The component that owns this controller
   */
  private host: FormResetCapable & Element;

  /**
   * The form manager responsible for this component's form
   */
  private manager?: FormResetManager;

  constructor(host: FormResetCapable & Element) {
    this.host = host;
    host.addController(this);
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is connected to the DOM
   * Sets up form reset registration
   */
  hostConnected(): void {
    this.registerWithForm();
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is disconnected from the DOM
   * Cleans up form reset registration
   */
  hostDisconnected(): void {
    this.unregisterFromForm();
  }

  /**
   * Called after the host component has been updated
   * Not needed for form reset functionality
   */
  hostUpdated(): void {
    // No action needed for form reset
  }

  /**
   * Registers this component with its containing form's reset manager
   * Creates a new manager if this is the first component in the form
   */
  private registerWithForm(): void {
    const form = this.host.closest('form');
    if (!form) return;

    // Get or create form manager
    let manager = FormResetController.formManagers.get(form);
    if (!manager) {
      manager = new FormResetManager(form, () => {
        FormResetController.formManagers.delete(form);
      });
      FormResetController.formManagers.set(form, manager);
    }

    manager.registerComponent(this.host);
    this.manager = manager;
  }

  /**
   * Unregisters this component from its form's reset manager
   */
  private unregisterFromForm(): void {
    if (this.manager) {
      this.manager.unregisterComponent(this.host);
      this.manager = undefined;
    }
  }
}

/**
 * Internal class that manages form reset for a single form
 * Maintains a set of components and handles the form reset event
 */
class FormResetManager {
  /**
   * Set of components registered with this form
   * Using Set ensures no duplicate registrations
   */
  private components = new Set<FormResetCapable>();

  /**
   * Bound event handler to maintain correct 'this' context
   */
  private resetHandler = this.handleFormReset.bind(this);

  constructor(
    private form: HTMLFormElement,
    private onEmpty: () => void
  ) {
    // Add single event listener for this form
    this.form.addEventListener('reset', this.resetHandler);
  }

  /**
   * Registers a component with this form manager
   * @param component - The component to register for reset notifications
   */
  registerComponent(component: FormResetCapable): void {
    this.components.add(component);
  }

  /**
   * Unregisters a component from this form manager
   * @param component - The component to unregister
   */
  unregisterComponent(component: FormResetCapable): void {
    this.components.delete(component);

    // If no more components, clean up the event listener
    if (this.components.size === 0) {
      this.form.removeEventListener('reset', this.resetHandler);
      this.onEmpty();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Handles the form reset event by notifying all registered components
   */
  private handleFormReset(): void {
    // Notify all registered components about the reset
    this.components.forEach(component => {
      component._handleFormReset();
    });
  }
}



================================================
FILE: src/config/controllers/form-validation.controller.ts
================================================
import { ReactiveController, ReactiveControllerHost } from 'lit';

/**
 * Interface that form components must implement to use FormValidationController
 */
export interface FormValidationCapable extends ReactiveControllerHost {
  /**
   * Method called when the form containing this component is submitted
   * Components must implement this to define their validation behavior
   * @returns boolean indicating if the component is valid
   */
  _handleFormSubmitValidation(): boolean;
}

/**
 * A controller that adds form validation functionality to form components
 *
 * This controller ensures that only one submit event listener is added per form,
 * regardless of how many form components are in the form. It uses a static
 * registry to track forms and their associated components.
 */
export class FormValidationController implements ReactiveController {
  /**
   * Static registry mapping forms to their form validation managers
   * Using WeakMap ensures automatic cleanup when forms are garbage collected
   */
  private static formManagers = new WeakMap<HTMLFormElement, FormValidationManager>();

  /**
   * The component that owns this controller
   */
  private host: FormValidationCapable & Element;

  /**
   * The form manager responsible for this component's form
   */
  private manager?: FormValidationManager;

  constructor(host: FormValidationCapable & Element) {
    this.host = host;
    host.addController(this);
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is connected to the DOM
   * Sets up form validation registration
   */
  hostConnected(): void {
    this.registerWithForm();
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is disconnected from the DOM
   * Cleans up form validation registration
   */
  hostDisconnected(): void {
    this.unregisterFromForm();
  }

  /**
   * Called after the host component has been updated
   * Not needed for form validation functionality
   */
  hostUpdated(): void {
    // No action needed for form validation
  }

  /**
   * Registers this component with its containing form's validation manager
   * Creates a new manager if this is the first component in the form
   */
  private registerWithForm(): void {
    const form = this.host.closest('form');
    if (!form) return;

    // Get or create form manager
    let manager = FormValidationController.formManagers.get(form);
    if (!manager) {
      manager = new FormValidationManager(form, () => {
        FormValidationController.formManagers.delete(form);
      });
      FormValidationController.formManagers.set(form, manager);
    }

    manager.registerComponent(this.host);
    this.manager = manager;
  }

  /**
   * Unregisters this component from its form's validation manager
   */
  private unregisterFromForm(): void {
    if (this.manager) {
      this.manager.unregisterComponent(this.host);
      this.manager = undefined;
    }
  }
}

/**
 * Internal class that manages form validation for a single form
 * Maintains a set of components and handles the form submit event
 */
class FormValidationManager {
  /**
   * Set of components registered with this form
   * Using Set ensures no duplicate registrations
   */
  private components = new Set<FormValidationCapable>();

  /**
   * Bound event handler to maintain correct 'this' context
   */
  private submitHandler = this.handleFormSubmit.bind(this);

  constructor(
    private form: HTMLFormElement,
    private onEmpty: () => void
  ) {
    // Add single event listener for this form
    this.form.addEventListener('submit', this.submitHandler, { capture: true });
  }

  /**
   * Registers a component with this form manager
   * @param component - The component to register for validation notifications
   */
  registerComponent(component: FormValidationCapable): void {
    this.components.add(component);
  }

  /**
   * Unregisters a component from this form manager
   * @param component - The component to unregister
   */
  unregisterComponent(component: FormValidationCapable): void {
    this.components.delete(component);

    // If no more components, clean up the event listener
    if (this.components.size === 0) {
      this.form.removeEventListener('submit', this.submitHandler, { capture: true });
      this.onEmpty();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Handles the form submit event by validating all registered components
   * Prevents submission if any component is invalid
   */
  private handleFormSubmit(event: Event): void {
    let isFormValid = true;
    let firstInvalidElement: Element | null = null;

    // Validate all registered components
    this.components.forEach(component => {
      const isComponentValid = component._handleFormSubmitValidation();
      if (!isComponentValid && isFormValid) {
        isFormValid = false;
        // Store the element for focusing later
        if (component instanceof Element) {
          firstInvalidElement = component;
        }
      }
    });

    // If any component is invalid, prevent form submission and focus the first invalid field
    if (!isFormValid && !event.defaultPrevented) {
      event.preventDefault();
      // Use type assertion to access focus method
      if (firstInvalidElement) {
        (firstInvalidElement as HTMLElement).focus();
      }
    }
  }
}

FILE: src/config/controllers/link-handler.controller.ts
================================================
import { ReactiveController, ReactiveControllerHost } from 'lit';

/**
 * A controller that adds link handling functionality to a component
 */
export class LinkHandlerController implements ReactiveController {
  /**
   * The host element that this controller is attached to
   */
  private host: ReactiveControllerHost & Element;

  /**
   * The URL that the link points to
   */
  public href = '';

  /**
   * Whether the link should open in a new tab
   */
  public newTab = false;

  /**
   * Whether the link is for downloading content
   */
  public downloadable = false;

  /**
   * The filename to use when downloading content
   */
  public filename = '';

  /**
   * Whether the link is disabled
   */
  public disabled = false;

  constructor(host: ReactiveControllerHost & Element) {
    this.host = host;
    host.addController(this);
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is connected to the DOM
   * Sets up click event listener for link handling
   */
  hostConnected(): void {
    this.host.addEventListener('click', this.handleClick.bind(this));
    this.updateHostAttributes();
  }

  /**
   * Called when the host component is disconnected from the DOM
   * Cleans up event listeners
   */
  hostDisconnected(): void {
    this.host.removeEventListener('click', this.handleClick.bind(this));
  }

  /**
   * Called after the host component has been updated
   * Updates host attributes when link properties change
   */
  hostUpdated(): void {
    this.updateHostAttributes();
  }

  /**
   * Handles click events on the host element
   */
  private handleClick(event: Event): void {
    if (this.disabled || !this.href) {
      event.preventDefault();
      return;
    }

    const sanitizedHref = this.sanitizeUrl(this.href);
    if (!sanitizedHref) {
      event.preventDefault();
      return;
    }

    // Handle downloadable content
    if (this.downloadable) {
      // Let the browser handle the download naturally
      return;
    }

    // Handle external URLs or new tab requests
    const isExternal =
      /^https?:\/\//.test(sanitizedHref) && !sanitizedHref.includes(window.location.hostname);

    if (isExternal || this.newTab) {
      window.open(sanitizedHref, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer');
      event.preventDefault();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Updates the host element's attributes based on link properties
   */
  private updateHostAttributes(): void {
    // Only set link attributes if the host has an href or is meant to be a link
    const shouldBeLink = this.href && !this.disabled;

    // Set cursor style only
    if (shouldBeLink) {
      (this.host as unknown as HTMLElement).style.cursor = 'pointer';
    } else {
      (this.host as unknown as HTMLElement).style.cursor = 'default';
    }
  }

  /**
   * Sanitizes a URL to prevent XSS attacks
   *
   * @param url - The URL to sanitize
   * @returns The sanitized URL
   */
  sanitizeUrl(url: string): string {
    // Trim and lowercase for consistent checks
    const sanitized = url.trim().toLowerCase();

    // Block javascript: and data: URLs which can lead to XSS
    if (
      sanitized.startsWith('javascript:') ||
      sanitized.startsWith('data:') ||
      sanitized.includes('\0')
    ) {
      return '';
    }

    // Only allow specific protocols and relative URLs
    if (
      !sanitized.startsWith('http://') &&
      !sanitized.startsWith('https://') &&
      !sanitized.startsWith('mailto:') &&
      !sanitized.startsWith('tel:') &&
      !sanitized.startsWith('/') &&
      !sanitized.startsWith('#')
    ) {
      return sanitized.startsWith('://') ? '' : `https://${url}`;
    }

    return url;
  }

  /**
   * Gets the appropriate attributes for a link element
   *
   * @returns A record of attribute names and values
   */
  getLinkAttributes(): Record<string, string> {
    const attributes: Record<string, string> = {};

    if (!this.href || this.disabled) {
      return attributes;
    }

    const sanitizedHref = this.sanitizeUrl(this.href);
    attributes['href'] = sanitizedHref;

    // Handle external URLs
    const isExternal =
      /^https?:\/\//.test(sanitizedHref) && !sanitizedHref.includes(window.location.hostname);

    if (isExternal || this.newTab) {
      attributes['target'] = '_blank';
      attributes['rel'] = 'noopener noreferrer';
    }

    // Handle downloadable content
    if (this.downloadable) {
      attributes['download'] = this.filename || '';
      // Best practice to still include security attributes
      attributes['rel'] = 'noopener noreferrer';
    }

    // Add accessibility attributes for non-anchor elements
    if (this.host.tagName.toLowerCase() !== 'a') {
      attributes['role'] = 'link';
      attributes['tabindex'] = '0';
    }

    return attributes;
  }
}

FILE: src/config/controllers/list-menu.controller.ts
================================================
import type { ReactiveController, ReactiveControllerHost } from 'lit';
import { ListMenuContextValue } from '@/config/contexts';
export class ListMenuController implements ReactiveController {
  private getContext: () => ListMenuContextValue | undefined;

  hostConnected(): void {
    // Optional: logic to run when host is connected
  }

  hostDisconnected(): void {
    // Optional: logic to run when host is disconnected
  }

  constructor(host: ReactiveControllerHost, getContext: () => ListMenuContextValue | undefined) {
    this.getContext = getContext;
    host.addController(this);
  }

  get role(): 'option' | 'menuitem' {
    return this.getContext()?.role === 'menu' ? 'menuitem' : 'option';
  }

  get multiSelectable(): boolean {
    return !!this.getContext()?.multiSelectable;
  }

  getAriaSelected(selected: boolean): string | null {
    return this.role === 'option' ? String(selected) : null;
  }

  getTabIndex(disabled: boolean): number {
    return disabled ? -1 : 0;
  }

  handleKeyNavigation(
    e: KeyboardEvent,
    items: HTMLElement[],
    currentIndex: number,
    onClick: () => void
  ): void {
    if (!items.length || currentIndex === -1) return;

    let nextIndex = -1;
    switch (e.key) {
      case 'ArrowDown':
        e.preventDefault();
        nextIndex = (currentIndex + 1) % items.length;
        break;
      case 'ArrowUp':
        e.preventDefault();
        nextIndex = (currentIndex - 1 + items.length) % items.length;
        break;
      case 'Enter':
      case ' ':
        onClick();
        return;
      default:
        return;
    }

    const nextItem = items[nextIndex];
    const focusTarget =
      (nextItem?.shadowRoot?.querySelector('[tabindex="0"]') as HTMLElement) ?? null;
    focusTarget?.focus();
  }
}

FILE: src/config/decorators/meta.decorator.ts
================================================
import { ColibriElements } from '@/types';

interface MetaOptions {
  name: string;
  tokensName?: string;
  components?: ColibriElements[];
}

export function Meta(options: MetaOptions) {
  return function <T extends { new (...args: any[]): {} }>(target: T) {
    return class extends target {
      static readonly tagName = options.name;
      static readonly components = options.components;
      tokensName = options.tokensName || options.name;

      constructor(...args: any[]) {
        super(...args);
      }
    };
  };
}

FILE: src/config/decorators/validate.decorator.test.ts

import { expect } from '@open-wc/testing';
import { Validate } from './validate.decorator';

describe('Validate decorator', () => {
  let originalWarn: any;
  let warnCalls: any[];

  beforeEach(() => {
    warnCalls = [];
    originalWarn = console.warn;
    console.warn = (...args: any[]) => {
      warnCalls.push(args);
    };
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    console.warn = originalWarn;
  });

  describe('Basic validation', () => {
    it('should accept valid values', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['red', 'green', 'blue'], 'red')
        color!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.color = 'blue';
      expect(instance.color).to.equal('blue');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(0);
    });

    it('should reject invalid values and use default', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['red', 'green', 'blue'], 'red')
        color!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.color = 'yellow';
      expect(instance.color).to.equal('red');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
      expect(warnCalls[0][0]).to.include('Invalid value "yellow"');
      expect(warnCalls[0][0]).to.include('Falling back to "red"');
    });

    it('should return default value when property is not set', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['small', 'medium', 'large'], 'medium')
        size!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();
      expect(instance.size).to.equal('medium');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(0);
    });
  });

  describe('Property behavior', () => {
    it('should maintain valid values after setting', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate([1, 2, 3], 1)
        number!: number;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.number = 2;
      expect(instance.number).to.equal(2);

      instance.number = 3;
      expect(instance.number).to.equal(3);

      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(0);
    });

    it('should allow multiple invalid assignments', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['a', 'b', 'c'], 'a')
        letter!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.letter = 'x';
      expect(instance.letter).to.equal('a');

      instance.letter = 'y';
      expect(instance.letter).to.equal('a');

      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(2);
    });

    it('should handle valid assignment after invalid one', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['red', 'green', 'blue'], 'red')
        color!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.color = 'yellow';
      expect(instance.color).to.equal('red');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);

      instance.color = 'green';
      expect(instance.color).to.equal('green');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
    });
  });

  describe('Custom warning messages', () => {
    it('should use custom warning message when provided', () => {
      const customMessage = (value: any, allowed: readonly any[]) =>
        `Custom error: "${value}" is not allowed. Use one of: ${allowed.join(', ')}`;

      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['red', 'green', 'blue'], 'red', customMessage)
        color!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.color = 'purple';
      expect(instance.color).to.equal('red');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
      expect(warnCalls[0][0]).to.equal(
        'Custom error: "purple" is not allowed. Use one of: red, green, blue'
      );
    });

    it('should pass correct parameters to custom warning function', () => {
      let capturedValue: any;
      let capturedAllowed: readonly any[];
      let callCount = 0;

      const customMessage = (value: any, allowed: readonly any[]) => {
        capturedValue = value;
        capturedAllowed = allowed;
        callCount++;
        return `Value: ${value}, Allowed: ${allowed.join(',')}`;
      };

      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['x', 'y', 'z'], 'x', customMessage)
        value!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = 'invalid';

      expect(callCount).to.equal(1);
      expect(capturedValue).to.equal('invalid');
      // @ts-ignore
      expect(capturedAllowed).to.deep.equal(['x', 'y', 'z']);
    });
  });

  describe('Multiple properties', () => {
    it('should handle multiple validated properties independently', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['red', 'green', 'blue'], 'red')
        color!: string;

        @Validate(['small', 'medium', 'large'], 'medium')
        size!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.color = 'green';
      instance.size = 'large';

      expect(instance.color).to.equal('green');
      expect(instance.size).to.equal('large');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(0);

      instance.color = 'invalid';
      instance.size = 'invalid';

      expect(instance.color).to.equal('red');
      expect(instance.size).to.equal('medium');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(2);
    });
  });

  describe('Data types', () => {
    it('should work with string values', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['alpha', 'beta', 'gamma'], 'alpha')
        value!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = 'beta';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('beta');

      instance.value = 'invalid';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('alpha');
    });

    it('should work with number values', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate([10, 20, 30], 10)
        value!: number;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = 20;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(20);

      instance.value = 15;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(10);
    });

    it('should work with boolean values', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate([true], true)
        value!: boolean;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = true;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(true);

      instance.value = false;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(true);
    });

    it('should work with mixed type arrays', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['auto', 0, false], 'auto')
        value!: string | number | boolean;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = 0;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(0);

      instance.value = false;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(false);

      instance.value = 'invalid';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('auto');
    });
  });

  describe('Edge cases', () => {
    it('should handle null and undefined values', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate([null, 'valid'], null)
        value!: string | null;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = null;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(null);

      instance.value = undefined as any;
      expect(instance.value).to.equal(null);
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
    });

    it('should handle empty allowed values array', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate([], 'default')
        value!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      expect(instance.value).to.equal('default');

      instance.value = 'anything';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('default');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
    });

    it('should handle single allowed value', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['only'], 'only')
        value!: string;
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();

      instance.value = 'only';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('only');

      instance.value = 'other';
      expect(instance.value).to.equal('only');
      expect(warnCalls.length).to.equal(1);
    });
  });

  describe('Property descriptor properties', () => {
    it('should make property enumerable', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['a', 'b'], 'a')
        value!: string;
      }

      const descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(TestClass.prototype, 'value');

      expect(descriptor?.enumerable).to.be.true;
    });

    it('should make property configurable', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['a', 'b'], 'a')
        value!: string;
      }

      const descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(TestClass.prototype, 'value');

      expect(descriptor?.configurable).to.be.true;
    });

    it('should include property in object enumeration', () => {
      class TestClass {
        @Validate(['a', 'b'], 'a')
        value!: string;

        normalProperty = 'normal';
      }

      const instance = new TestClass();
      instance.value = 'a';

      const keys = Object.keys(instance);
      expect(keys).to.include('normalProperty');
    });
  });
});

FILE: src/config/decorators/validate.decorator.ts
================================================
/**
 * A decorator factory that creates a property decorator which validates property values
 * against a list of allowed values and provides a fallback if validation fails.
 *
 * @param allowedValues - Array of allowed values for the property
 * @param defaultValue - Fallback value to use when validation fails
 * @param warningMessage - Optional custom warning message format
 * @returns A property decorator
 */
export function Validate<T>(
  allowedValues: readonly T[],
  defaultValue: T,
  warningMessage?: (value: any, allowed: readonly T[]) => string
) {
  return function (target: any, propertyKey: string) {
    // Property storage key
    const storageKey = Symbol(`__${propertyKey}`);

    // Create property definition
    Object.defineProperty(target, propertyKey, {
      get() {
        return this[storageKey] !== undefined ? this[storageKey] : defaultValue;
      },
      set(value: any) {
        if (allowedValues.includes(value)) {
          this[storageKey] = value;
        } else {
          // Value is invalid, use default and log warning
          this[storageKey] = defaultValue;

          // Use custom warning message if provided, otherwise use default format
          const message = warningMessage
            ? warningMessage(value, allowedValues)
            : `Invalid value "${value}" for property "${propertyKey}". Falling back to "${defaultValue}". Valid values are: ${allowedValues.join(', ')}`;

          console.warn(message);
        }
      },
      enumerable: true,
      configurable: true,
    });
  };
}

FILE: src/config/mixins/form-component.mixin.ts
================================================
import { LitElement } from 'lit';
import { FormResetMixin, Constructor } from './form-reset.mixin.js';
import { FormValidationMixin } from './form-validation.mixin.js';

/**
 * A comprehensive mixin that adds both form reset and validation capabilities
 * to a component. This creates a complete form-aware component.
 *
 * @example
 * ```typescript
 * export class MyFormField extends FormComponentMixin(LitElement) {
 *   // Implement required methods
 *   _handleFormReset(): void {
 *     this.value = '';
 *     this.touched = false;
 *   }
 *
 *   _handleFormSubmitValidation(): boolean {
 *     this._updateValidState();
 *     return this.valid;
 *   }
 * }
 * ```
 */
export const FormComponentMixin = <T extends Constructor<LitElement>>(superClass: T) => {
  // Apply both mixins in sequence
  return FormValidationMixin(FormResetMixin(superClass));
};
